Title: A World Forgotten
Chapter_Text:
Third Person's POV:

Shubman Gill, a renowned cricketer, woke up in a hospital bed after suffering an injury, details of which were unknown to everyone. His family and friends were by his side throughout the tough times. A team of elite doctors was there to treat him and get him back in cricket, and although he was out of danger, he didn't wake up until now, weeks after the incident.

When Shubman woke up, he found his surroundings to be strange. The first person he saw was his elder sister, Shahneel. The white walls of the hospital made his vision blurry, and when he tried to speak, his voice betrayed him. It was as if he had forgotten how to speak. The strong smell of medicine and cleaning solution overwhelmed him, and he fell back into the bed with a thud.

This caught Shahneel's attention, and she quickly came over to her brother, holding his hand and pressing the call button with her other hand. Two minutes later, a team of nurses entered the room with concerned expressions, but they visibly relaxed when they saw Shubman looking around with his eyes wide open. "Di, what happened?" he innocently asked Shahneel.

"You got yourself into an accident, Shubi. But don't worry, you will be alright to play again in a few months." "But I have practice sessions; the coach won't take me to the upcoming tournament if I miss those." He said, confusing Shahneel quite a bit. "No..there is just IPL coming up, and I am sure until the end of IPL you will be alright again. I think you can miss one IPL Shubi." Shahneel said sweetly, but her words had left a visible frown on Shubman's forehead. He looked at his sister like she had grown a second head.

"Why would you say that? What IPL?" he asked, confused. Shahneel didn't understand the depth of the situation and left it for the doctors to let her know the reason behind her brother talking nonsense again.

Later, when the doctor came in for an examination, Shahneel brought up the incident from the morning, making the doctor frown too. He had immediately written Shubman a few tests and was waiting for the reports to arrive. "Is it concerning?" Shahneel asked. "I think there might have been some problems with his memory, but since he remembers you, I don't understand the severity of this memory loss." "WHAT?"

A memory loss, Shahneel thought, would make quite an impact on Shubman's career because how the fuck would he play like that? But he does remember me, so it cannot be that bad, right? Her thoughts were cut short when the doctor called her in. "Shubman, what is the last thing that you remember?" The doctor asked Shubman; he frowned at the question and looked at his sister. "Nothing. Can I go to the camp again, please?" he asked them, making them both look at each other. Concerned.

"What camp?" Shahneel asked, grabbing Shubman's attention. "The cricketing camp di. Did you forget?" "Shubi, what year is it?" Shahneel asked him, sensing trouble. "2014?" he answered, shocking both the people present in the room. "No shubi, it is 2024." "WHAT?"

After the incident, the doctor suggested that Shubman should meet people he might not have met until 2014 and bond with them. According to him, this could help bring back his memory. The doctor assured that it was not a permanent memory loss, so there was nothing to worry about. He explained that it could be due to adrenaline blocking certain memories to focus on survival, or it could be due to concussion from the accident, but either way, there was nothing too permanent to worry about.

The doctor also advised that the details of the accident were not to be discussed in front of Shubman too early, before his brain was ready to process them. The next day, his mother and father, who had been in town while Shubman was admitted, came in to meet him. The nurses all treated Shubman like he was some celebrity, and he couldn't process that information. He tried asking his sister about it, but she didn't give him any details at all.

"He hasn't come in for a while now? Did they inform him about Shubman's improvement?" He overheard the nurses outside his door talking about someone he had no idea about. This all seemed very strange to Shubman.

It took him quite some time to process the change in his body after waking up. He didn't remember becoming this muscular or his facial features changing so much. To him, it felt like it was just yesterday that he was about 14 or 15 years old. A ten-year jump in time was a lot to take in.

He watched some videos of himself performing online and couldn't believe that he was playing with people he could only dream about playing with a few years ago. This new information made him very happy, but he couldn't shake the feeling of missing out on something. He felt the need to meet someone, but he didn't know who this someone was.

The days passed by in a blur, and suddenly a week had gone by since he had woken up. His family had decided to keep this information a secret and only inform people about his awakening after returning home. This was a precaution to avoid any potential stalkers or paparazzi. Even people from cricket and Shubman's closest friends were not informed about his waking up.

"Ishan? Shouldn't we tell him?" Shahneel asked her father one evening. He looked at her, shocked. "You didn't tell him? I thought that you did." "No, you said that you didn't want anyone to know just yet." "Well, stupid girl, that obviously didn't include Ishan." Shubman looked at his father with raised eyebrows. He had no idea who they were talking about or why he was so special, but he figured out that he must have been someone important.

"Why didn't he come in these past few days though?" His father asked Shahneel. "Well, he came in just a day before Shubman woke up, and he was supposed to be going in for some tour after that; he refused obviously, but I forced him to go. I didn't want to tell him also because then he would just return in the middle of it all." "Right. So, on second thought, let him just complete his work. Don't tell him." "Alright then, I will go inform Pallavi Di about it," Shahneel said and left the room.

Her father left behind her to get some medicines from the pharmacy, leaving behind a very confused and concerned Shubman. He tried to pressure his brain and think about any Ishan that he might be close enough to for his family to be talking about like that, but he seemed to have recalled no one as such. He gave up thinking after some time.

After that encounter, the Ishan guy was not mentioned again in front of Shubman, and he didn't think about the incident anymore either. It had been a little over a week since Shubman had woken up, and he was ruled fit enough to continue his physiotherapy in the comfort of his home. And so, he was discharged from the hospital.

The watchful gaze of people didn't bother him as much as it used to, and he was now preparing himself to enter a world that had nothing to do with the Shubman he had woken up as. Thinking about the difficulties that he might have to face in the future churned his stomach, but looking at his family, he thought that no matter how much time it would take, everything would eventually be alright again.

While his family was standing at the reception, completing the formalities for the discharge, Shubman was busy thinking about things again. He was brought back to the reality of everything when someone mentioned the date today. "It is 18th July, isn't it?" he heard and immediately looked in the direction.

This sudden move of his seemed to have alerted his family, and they looked at him concerned. "What is wrong, Shubi?" his mother asked in that sweet tone of hers. "18th July?" Shubman asked her. She frowned, but then she seemed to have remembered something and immediately looked at her husband. Shahneel was also looking at Shubman with wide eyes.

"Do you remember something?" she asked him, looking hopeful, "No. It's just that. Isn't it some special day? There is something today, right?" "You think there is something special on 18th July?" Shahneel asked him softly, as if she were speaking to a toddler. "There isn't?" Shubman asked, feeling embarrassed, but he could have sworn that there was something special today.

Although he didn't know what it was, he knew that it wasn't a day to be forgotten easily; it was as if the date was printed at the back of his brain and initiated instant reactions from him. Shahneel was still looking at him with soft eyes that he didn't know the meaning of. "Why?" he asked his sister, but she just smiled in return and said, "It is actually someone's birthday, Shubi. Someone that you know."

He tried asking her about who this person was and why his birthday was so important that he remembered it even after he had forgotten almost everything else. He also thought about the possibility that it wasn't special because it was someone's birthday, but maybe there was something else entirely. But his heart was firm with the truth that his sister provided but refused to elaborate on. Every time he tried asking her about it, she would just smile and shake her head.

It was all too new for Shubman, and he still hadn't entered his forgotten world yet. It would be a long journey, he thought.

Title: Do I Know Him?
Chapter_Text:
Today Shubman's family was all busy trying to convince someone not to go somewhere, leaving something behind, and Shubman was very confused. He tried asking about it, but they were all too busy. After the nth attempt, Shubman gave up and returned to the confines of his room, where he sat and thought about life again.

Shubman had no work these days. He ate, slept, watched something, and slept again. That was all he did, with a bit of physiotherapy and a hell lot of medicine, of course. Some people came in to meet him every day. He knew most of them. The people that he had forgotten about were all busy with something and couldn't make it to him at the moment. Something told him that today he will be meeting one of them. Would it be Virat Kohli? There was anticipation in his heart; he was a mere fan after all.

What wouldn't he have given to be playing for Team India all those years back? This was him living his dream, and he was proud of himself for it. But when he thought about it some more, he realized that this memory loss thing could scar his cricket career permanently, and that thought was scary; it was more frightening to the 15-year-old shubman inside of him. So, Shubman was trying his best to regain his memories, but every time he tried remembering something, it would trigger a non-existent migraine in his head, and he would be discouraged to continue.

Shubman was battling another episode of migraines when he heard a commotion at the entrance of his house. Curious, he stood up and walked downstairs. As he descended halfway, he noticed a man standing at the gate, looking concerned. Surrounded by Shubman's family members, the man seemed to be searching for someone.

Their eyes met, and Shubman felt a spark of recognition. Although he didn't know who the man was or why he was there, he had a strange sense of familiarity. As Shubman descended the stairs, the man broke free from the crowd and hurried towards him, pulling him into a warm but urgent hug. Shubman was stunned.

Although he didn't know who this man was, the hug made him feel secure, like he had finally found something he was desperately looking for, something that had been lost for some time now. The man broke free from the hug and looked down, embarrassed. "Sorry, are you okay, Shubi?" he asked him, and he realized that they were on a nickname basis. "Shubi?" Shubman repeated dumbly.

"Ishan, we wanted to tell you something, but you wouldn't listen." Shahneel started, and Shubman looked at her, trying to figure out the words that would be coming out of her mouth next. Shubman was thrilled to know that this pretty man was the Ishan everyone was talking about. Ishan looked at Shahneel too, waiting for her to continue. Even from a distance, Shubman could feel Ishan's heart racing. He was nervous.

"Shubman has lost his memories." Shahneel gave the jab at one go and waited for her words to sink in. "what?" Ishan looked baffled, like he had anticipated everything in the world but this. He looked around, trying to figure something out. "Shubman doesn't remember anything that happened in the past ten years. The last thing he remembers is from when he was 14 or 15." Shubman's mother said, trying to help Ishan. "You don't remember me?" He asked, looking at Shubman, and the look he received proved that Shubman actually didn't have any recollection of him at all.

Ishan was then brought into the living room, where he was fed like a toddler. Ishan refused to eat much, saying that he had an upset stomach, but Shubman's family knew better than that. Shubman got wind of one thing: Ishan was a cricketer too. His family had informed Ishan about Shubman waking up in the middle of one of his tournaments, and Ishan had hurried here, ditching everything else. This was what the commotion was about. Shahneel had given Ishan an earful about how he shouldn't have done that and how that was the reason they didn't want him to know about it even earlier.

Ishan took it all quite sportingly. He had blended in with them like it was the most natural thing in the world, and Shubman was left in awe. His mother, he realized, had called Ishan "son" for the fifth time in a row, and the irony of it all was that if an outsider were to be added to this picture, he wouldn't say that Ishan didn't belong in this family. Shubman was left too stunned by it all.

Shaheel passed Shubman a smirk when she caught him staring at Ishan; the meaning of that smirk was beyond Shubman's understanding, so he remained quiet. Ishan gave Shubman a small smile and asked him if he was okay for the second time this evening. This time Shubman decided to answer him. "Yes, I am very well; thank you." "Goddd shubman, I know that you don't remember me, but please, for the love of everything precious, don't be formal with me."

Shubman just smiled at that. He could feel Ishan cringing at his formal tone. They might have been really close, he realized. "How old are you?" Shubman asked out of nowhere, stunning everyone present at the table. "a year older than you." Shahneel decided to answer instead. Shubman just nodded at that.

When his family was talking about Ishan back at the hospital, he thought that he might be someone important to them, but looking at their bond now, this was way beyond just being important to them. Ishan even knew all of Shubman's house-helps by their first names, and everybody seemed to adore him. Ishan had brought in a wave of happiness and hope with him, and although Shubman didn't recognize him, the fact that he had someone like him in his life warmed his heart.

After that encounter with Ishan, Shubman had stormed his brain for any possible explanation for the pull he felt with him. Shubman had realized that he definitely knew him from somewhere and that he was important to him, but where did he know him from? How had they met? All of these questions had helped catalyze his migraine, but he refused to just give up.

Shubman had heard that voice before; he had seen that face before, from where, he had no idea. Shubman had decided to finally give his paining head a bit of rest and go ask Shahneel about Ishan. He had imagined certain answers or even a lack of response from his sister; what he hadn't anticipated though was that brilliant smile that his sister passed him when he mentioned Ishan's name.

She smiled at him like she was waiting for him to approach her, like she had just won a bet against someone. That smile scarred Shubman more than it comforted him. "What?" she smiled again. "You want to know about Ishan, Shubi?" She said it in that vomit-inducing sweet tone that Shubman absolutely hated. Anything that followed that tone was always a disaster.

Shubman gave Shahneel an unimpressed look and proceeded to leave her room. Shahneel, on the other hand, caught Shubman's forearm and dragged him inside again. "Alright, alright, no more teasing." She continued when they had both settled on her bed. "Ishan, his name is Ishan; that should turn some gears in your head shubman, and if it doesn't, then I am very concerned. He was your best friend." "Is." Shubman interrupted her before she could continue.

Shahneel looked at him with curious eyes but decided to continue nonetheless. "yeah, is. He is your best friend. He was the one we were most afraid of telling about you. He had been stuck in the hospital since the day you were first admitted and absolutely refused to go on that tour of his. I had to drag his ass out of the hospital and into an Uber. It just slipped up by my stupid mouth during one of our phone calls, that you had woken up. And that madman took a flight here."

Shubman just smiled at her. Listening to her, Shubman felt like it was something that he would do. "I just told him that you woke up and he cancelled my call and rushed here; I couldn't even tell him that you had lost your memories." She continued. "You'll seemed quite close with him," Shubman said, searching her eyes for answers.

"Well, his parents flew in to see you when you weren't even awake, so yeah, you can imagine your bond." She answered, making him think about it. "Oh, that reminds me, his parents would be here tomorrow. To meet you, of course. Although you don't remember them, please behave nicely." She said it pointedly. Shubman gave her a look at that. Of course, he would behave properly. Even if Ishan weren't his best friend and practically a member of his family, he would still behave nicely in front of his parents. He wasn't that stupid.

"Okay, okay, don't give me that look. Also, why were you curious about him?" "Well, you would be curious too if you suddenly lost your memory and some stranger came into your home and warmed up with your family like his own, and you have no idea why." He said it curtly, making Shahneel laugh in embarrassment. "Sorry, Shubi, I can't imagine what you must be going through at the moment. But remember that we will always be here, okay?" Sometimes Shubman liked his sister quite a lot.

He went to bed thinking about how and when he got to know this Ishan person and how they had become so close. He had this strong sense of familiarity with him, and his smile warmed his heart. With a single thought in his head, Shubman drifted off to sleep. 'Do I know him?'

Title: Meetings And Greetings
Chapter_Text:
The following day, as Shahneel had predicted, Ishan's parents along with his brother and sister-in-law visited Shubman's place. His family had been preparing for their arrival since the day before. Just when Shubman thought he had seen everything, he was surprised to see his sister making a special effort to welcome the guests. He wondered if she was okay.

Shubman sat on the living room sofa and watched his family bustling around the house doing chores. The doorbell rang twice, but no one paid any attention. After exchanging glances with his family, Shubman reluctantly got up to open the door. When he did, he found Ishan standing there. Their eyes met, and Ishan smiled, leaving Shubman momentarily breathless.

Shubman hadn't seen Ishan up close yesterday and now he regretted being the one to open the door. He was stuck on the spot and really wanted to step aside to let Ishan inside, but his feet just wouldn't move. Shahneel fed up with her brother's antics, pushed Shubman aside and asked Ishan to come in.

Ishan entered the house and gave Shubman a heartwarming smile. He smiled back, but that made Ishan's eyes look sad for some reason. Shubman wanted to ask Ishan about it, but Ishan was pulled into a conversation by his father. Shubman realized that Ishan's family would be here until noon. After a good 30 minutes, Ishan was left unattended just like Shubman, and Shubman's family restarted whatever shenanigans they were doing before.

"Hello. I couldn't speak with you yesterday. It seems like we know each other very well," Shubman tried to start a conversation. There was a shine in Ishan's eyes when he saw Shubman initiating a conversation with him, and Shubman felt this urgent need to protect that shine forever. "We do, actually. We were best friends." The use of 'were' bothered Shubman a bit too much. Why was everyone hell-bent on using the past tense?

"Aren't we still best friends?" he asked, making Ishan smile sweetly at him. "Yes, Shubi, we still are best friends. It's just that I didn't know if you would still want to be my best friend. Sorry." Shubman wanted to punch himself for any reason why Ishan would be thinking like that. "I am sure I would always want you to be my best friend, Ishan. My family doesn't act like this with any of my friends. You have to be someone special."

Was that a blush on Ishan's cheeks? Shubman thought that it was the most adorable thing in the world. He was going to say something when the doorbell rang, interrupting their blissful bubble. When his sister opened the door, it revealed four people and a toddler. This was presumably Ishan's family.

Shubman got up to greet them when the toddler ran towards him, smiling happily. That smile reminded him of Ishan a bit too much. "Shubman chachuuu." And with that, he jumped into Shubman's arms. Shubman caught him but looked at his sister, searching for answers. "He is Viraj, Raj Bhaiya's son," Ishan added helpfully. "Viraj, come to Chachu, come on," Ishan tried to take Viraj from Shubman, but that child just refused to budge. Shubman just smiled at the gesture. He was practically clinging to Shubman for dear life while Ishan was struggling.

"No, it's fine. Let him be," Shubman said while he went towards Ishan's family. With Viraj still in his arms, Shubman bent down to touch the feet of Ishan's parents and take blessings from them. They weren't shocked at this gesture one bit. It seemed like he did so often enough for them to not be surprised anymore. "Kaisa hai beta?" his mother asked Shubman. He just smiled at her and nodded his head.

They were all soon settled in the living room, and the air was lively again. Shubman was just looking around, happy about having such amazing people in his life. Ishan was still trying to get Viraj off Shuman, but that child was too stubborn. "Shubman Chachu, when will you revisit our house?" he asked, looking too innocent. "He won't visit if you don't get off of him this instant, Viraj," Ishan said getting frustrated by the situation. How can he be losing against a fucking child?

"I will visit soon." And then he said something in Viraj's ear that made him give Ishan a look and laugh. Ishan was glaring at Shubman when Pallavi came by and ripped Viraj off of Shubman and passed him a smile. "Abhi toh leave hogi na?" she asked him politely. "Haan, abhi leave hai. I don't know if I can return to cricket or not, though." "Of course, you will return," Ishan added almost instantly.

That made Pallavi and Shubman both look at him. Shubman was surprised, but Pallavi was looking at him lovingly. There was so much love in her eyes that Shubman felt it radiate through her. He, for some reason, was glad that Ishan had such loving people around him.

They all had lunch together, followed by a lot of family time. Looking at them, Shubman realized that it was not only him and Ishan who were close, but their families were close too. They all seemed too sweet. Ishan's mother had brought Shubman some dishes that he used to like. When Shubman tasted the food after they had left, he realized just why he liked it so much. It was delicious, and under no circumstance would he share it with anyone.

Days after that meeting went by in a blur for Shubman. He had nothing to do and nothing interesting to be looking forward to. He was treated like a sick person, and he was bored by all the activities that he so often did. His diet had just started, and his mood had become too easy to spoil because of it. What would a man do if not eat good food? The only guests that they had over these days were the doctors that Shubman despised so much.

Today though, Shubman felt like there was something to be looking forward to. He had woken up fresh to continue his daily routine half-heartedly, but life had different plans for him today. The moment he went downstairs, instead of coming face to face with his doctors, who had no interest in him, he came face to face with Ishan. He was pleasantly surprised. A bit too surprised.

So much so that he almost fell face-first in front of him but gathered himself up before embarrassing himself. But that was not all. When his attention was not on Ishan and only Ishan anymore, he realized that there were a lot more people in his living room today. When he looked closely, they were all the people he played cricket with. Some of them were easily recognizable, some it took him time to recognize (he had just seen them in the videos online).

His eyes roamed the room, trying to search for someone. He hurried towards him when he finally found him. "hello sir." Shubman told Virat when he had found his way towards him in the room full of people. Virat just smiled at him sweetly. Shaking his head, he said to no one in particular. "This reminds me so much of the Shubman that I first met." "Well.. this shubman is meeting his favorite idol for the first time too," Rohit said, smiling at Shubman and his never-ending love for Virat.

"Hello, dear Shubman, How are you doing?" Virat asked Shubman, messing with his hair adoringly. "I still can't believe that I play along with you." That sentence just slipped up, and when Shubman realized what he had said, he immediately clapped his mouth shut. This gesture made everyone in the room laugh. "Shubi Darling looks so innocent. It is making me want to vomit." Rishabh said, making Shubman glare at him.

"Well, the shubman ten years ago was innocent indeed, wasn't he?" Ishan added, his eyes shining. "true true." Someone agreed with him. "I still can't believe that he just lost his memories," Hardik added, looking horrified at the thought. And they all looked like they shared that thought. "I am sure that they will come back, and even if they don't, we can make more memories together. And make sure that they stay." Ishan said, calming the mood.

That sentence of Ishan made Shubman look at him. The look in Shubman's eyes was unreadable though, so Ishan just smiled at him. Shubman didn't reciprocate that smile. He looked like he was thinking about something; Shubman was pulled out of his thoughts when he heard some commotion around him and realized that everyone had scattered over in his house and was now busy doing something too unimportant.

They had all stayed with Shubman until the nightfall. They spoke about all the stupid stuff that Shubman had done, and Shubman was left rethinking his life choices during all of these. Even if they were a bunch of crackheads that liked to humiliate him, he still felt like all of his efforts were worth it after all. He got to play and spend the majority of his life with such amazing people, and it all felt too much like a dream to him.

"It's all true, Shubi; you've made it." He looked at Ishan, who was already looking at him. Ishan knew him a bit too well; Shubman realized. He finally knew why Ishan was his best friend. He understood him without him having to use words at all. He saw ishan smiling at him and Shubman returned his smile. And when their gaze was held, the world was sidelined for a moment.

After that moment, Shubman felt his gaze returning to Ishan, no matter what he was doing or whom he was talking to. He had no idea why though, and when he looked at Rishabh Again, whom he was currently having a conversation with, he saw him smirking at him. That smirk held the same meaning as the smirk of his sister, but he knew the interpretation of none of those smirks, and he was going to ask Rishabh about it but decided against it.

He enjoyed the day with his seemingly teammates a bit too much, and he instantly clicked with them. Maybe because he had subconsciously known them for quite some time now.

Title: The Party
Chapter_Text:
Shubman after meeting the Indian cricket team as his friend was on cloud nine. He was too happy to be asking any more questions to his sister. His medications were going well too. Doctors had advised him to spend more time with people he had presumably forgotten. That would, according to them, help in bringing his memories back. Shubman was too happy to spend time with the cricketers. To the 15-year-old him, it was the opportunity of a lifetime.

"Shubi, ishan ka call tha. Virat Bhai is throwing a party at his place today. He has invited you." His sister had informed him one fine evening, making Shubman's heart do a flip. He was getting invited to Virat Kohli's private party after all. The fan in him couldn't help but be happy.

"Why didn't he call me?" Shubman asked when the rational part of his brain started working again. "Yeah, I asked him that, but he was saying something about you feeling uncomfortable." "Uncomfortable? About?" Shubman asked, confused. I have no idea. "You would need to ask him about it." And with that, his sister went away to do something stupid again.

Shubman had been thinking about Ishan for quite some time now. Technically, he had been thinking about the life that he was living before the accident, and Ishan was unarguably a part of it. Hence, he was thinking about Ishan too. This weighed approach of Ishan had always bothered Shubman somehow. He didn't like the fact that Ishan thought that he would feel uncomfortable if he called him.

He had made up his mind to confront Ishan at this party. Tell him that he had no issues whatsoever. With that thought, he started to get himself ready for the party. The news of the party had a positive impact on his day. He was happy until it was time to leave; now he was anxious. Shubman suddenly realized that he would be meeting so many renowned figures there, and all of them would know him, but he, on the other hand, would not be able to recognize any of them. This thought made him feel anxious.

He had this sudden urge to bang his head somewhere just so he could get his memories back. He felt like he was missing out on so much. He put his coat on cursing the accident when he suddenly realized—right, the accident. What was it about? The doctor told his family to not tell him about it, but he didn't think that they would comply. But they did.

The thoughts in his head were put on hold when Shahneel knocked on his door. "Shubman, are you ready? Ishan is waiting downstairs." Ishan? He didn't say he would be coming? He came to do what? Pick him up? This thought made Shubman's heart do a little flip, and he had no idea why. He gathered his scattered thoughts and went downstairs, where Ishan was sitting waiting for him.

When Shubman saw Ishan from where he was standing upstairs, he looked immersed in a conversation with his mother, and the scene looked so serene that Shubman froze. Ishan was wearing a white shirt and a pair of blue jeans, with a pearl necklace around his neck. Nothing too fancy, yet he looked ethereal. The jeans were just the right fit, and the shirt had a couple of buttons undone at the top, revealing the pearl necklace. The necklace against his tan skin looked too good, and Shubman had to physically slap himself to get back to reality.

'What the fuck is wrong with me?' With that thought, Shubman went downstairs. He noticed his mother turning her head to look at him when she heard him come. He noticed Ishan's smile when their eyes met, but what he didn't notice was his sister's eyes that had mischief in them when she looked at him. Shubman missed the smile that Shahneel had plastered across her face.

He reached down and bent to get his mother's blessings. He was still trying to gather his thoughts that were wandering all around Ishan when Ishan said, "You are looking good in that suit, Shubi." There was a sweet smile on his face when he said that, but suddenly the smile disappeared, followed by an apology. "oh.. shubman. Right, Shubman. Sorry." That sorry had made Shubman's blood boil for some reason.

He grabbed Ishan's hand and left his house with Ishan on his heels. "what's wrong?" Ishan asked in that oh-so-innocent tone of his, "Your constant apologies and aloof nature is what is wrong. Can you not treat me normally? Look, I have no issues if you call me directly instead of calling Shahneel Di. In fact, I would appreciate it very much. Also, I have no issues if you call me Shubi. Can you treat me just like you did before I lost my memories, please?"

Ishan had a pretty smile on his face when he pulled Shubman into a bone-crushing hug. Shubman felt his heart flip again. This time, it was more intense than the last one. The speed at which his heart was beating, there was no way Ishan didn't hear it. "I would like my best friend back, but I didn't want to impose anything on you. I am sorry, and this is the last time I am apologizing for stupid stuff. I promise," Ishan said when he pulled apart from the hug.

Shubman had this urge to pull Ishan right back into that hug they had shared earlier, but he had a feeling that he shouldn't do it. Soon they were settled in the car to go attend the party. "Don't worry too much; all of the people invited have already met you. It is a secret, but Virat Bhai is actually arranging this party to help you regain your memories." Ishan said, smiling.

Ishan was driving them to the destination because Shubman obviously didn't know where to go. The thought of Virat arranging a whole damn party for Shubman had his heart melting. It was so thoughtful of him, but again, what else did he expect from the Virat Kohli? He had always been amazing in Schuman's subconscious, and proving his subconscious right was his part-time hobby.

The ride was silent, but it was not an awkward silence. Shubman was trying his best to not look at Ishan like a creep and scare him away. But he was looking so good that Shubman's eyes seemed to have a mind of their own. They returned to him every chance they got. There was no way Ishan didn't notice him staring, but he was so nonchalant about it that it made Shubman rethink what shit this man was used to putting up with.

They were greeted by Anushka at the gate. Shubman had recently found out that she got married to Virat. 'Not gonna lie, they looked stunning together' was what Shubman thought. "Ohooooo, look who is here. How are you, Shubi?" She pulled him in a hug and asked in the sweetest voice possible. "You brought him in alright?" She turned to Ishan with that. "Well, doesn't he look alright to you?" "He looks scared." "That is because he is standing in front of the Anushka Sharma," Ishan replied mischievously.

"Anushka Sharma Kohli, you mean?" There came Virat with a wide smile and hugged Anushka. He got elbowed in the stomach as a response. "Hello, mere sher, sab thik?" Virat asked Shubman once Anushka was inside the house again. "haa bhai. sab thik."  "Tu toh bada acha lagra hai. Sab ko behosh karne ka irada hai kya?" Virat asked teasingly, and then he turned to Ishan, "Kyun Ishan?"

"Bhai main pehli hi usko compliment de chuka hu." "Obviously, duh." That obviously didn't sit right with either of them. Both Shubman and Ishan looked confused at that. They looked at each other, shrugged their shoulders, and went inside.

They were slowly welcomed by everyone. Some of these people had not come to meet Shubman at his place earlier. And so, they were all busy asking him if he was alright. "Shubiiii, have a drink." It was Hardik who had taken the initiative of bringing alcohol for a patient. Shubman denied it once, but Hardik kept on insisting. Shubman couldn't understand how to interact with him like a normal person without making a complete fool of himself.

Just when he was about to take the drink with a grateful smile, someone else took the drink from Hardik's hand. "Hardik bhai? hospital se aaya hai who. Mana hai usko alcohol." It was Ishan. His tone was fierce. It almost sounded like a scold. "killjoy." Hardik muttered before scattering away.

Just then his eyes met Rishabh's, and he was back smirking at him again. "Why is Rishabh always giving me those looks?" he asked Ishan, who looked over at Rishabh, looking just as confused as him. "I have no idea. Did you do something?" Ishan asked him, to which he nodded in negative. What could have Shubman done in the countable 2 meetings with Rishabh to have the privilege to be receiving looks like that?

"You are looking good, Shubman." Rishabh had scooted over to him sometime earlier and was now initiating a conversation. "Thank you, Bhai. You are looking good yourself." "Well yes, thank you, but Ishan is looking better, isn't he?" Shubman nodded his head subconsciously before giving Rishabh a done look. He had no idea where this sudden topic of Ishan and his good looks was pulled from. But just when he looked at Rishabh, those smirks were back.

Shubman was confused, but he also had a feeling that Rishabh knew something he didn't remember. He wanted to ask Rishabh about it, but he didn't want a confrontation just yet. So, he decided to ignore Rishabh altogether.

Shubman was pulled onto the floor, where he was forced to dance. He loved every second of this party. He had made mental notes on a lot of people today, and he had a feeling that it would be really helpful in the near future. He was currently sitting on the living room sofa when Ishan came to him with a drink.

"What?" Shubman asked him, looking between him and the glass in his hands. "It's juice shubi, did you actually think that I would offer you anything other than that?" Shubman took the glass gratefully. "You drank?" Shubman asked him after a while. "no. I need to drive, remember?" Ishan replied, smiling.

Title: Jealous? No.
Chapter_Text:
Shubman was sitting next to Abhishek. He never expected to be so relieved to have Abhishek by his side. Honestly, Abhishek was the only familiar person here.

Abhishek, when he entered the room, made his way through the crowd directly towards him. Ishan looked like he was going to introduce Abhishek to Shubman, but Shubman just stared at him and then suddenly pulled Abhishek into a hug. Don't mind him; he was too happy to meet someone he knew.

Abhishek was smiling when they pulled apart. "I never expected our friendship to endure for such a long time." "rightttt. But see, we still are friends, and it's all thanks to me." "Obviously." And Shubman smiled with that. He looked beside him only to find Ishan smiling too. "I guess you are too happy to finally meet someone you remember knowing, right, Shubi?" He chuckled at that.

"Oh, Shubman, meet your new best friend. Ishan." There was that teasing smile on Abhishek's face that Shubman remembers witnessing when he introduced Shubman to some girl he thought was Shubman's type. The reason for the smile in this conversation was still unknown to Shubman. He passed Abhishek a done look and decided to pry no further into the topic.

"I have already met him. I think I now know why he is my best friend and not you." Abhishek raised his eyebrows at that and asked, "Oh. Why so?" "Because Abhi, this is not the first time I have met him after my accident." "Yeah, he has met you before, obviously. We were on a damn tour, okay, and I didn't have his guts to be leaving everything in the middle and running here to meet you." He rolled his eyes at the end, and Shubman smiled at the gesture.

"abhi." Ishan interrupted. "what? You know Shubman's health is the only reason you are not sacked yet, right? Why would you even do that? I swear they should have kept you tied up before telling you about him waking up because, the speed at which you ran, oh my god." "Yeah, because not everybody can be as unbothered as you, right?" Shubman said, raising his eyebrows with a small smile on his face.

The fact of Ishan running in the middle of his work to meet Shubman the moment he got the news of him waking up had always brought a smile on Shubman's face. "unbothered? Trust me, Shubman, everyone was dying to meet you, but, Ishan? Nope, nobody can be compared to him." "Okay enough." Ishan seemed to have run out of patience, and Abhishek instantly glued his mouth shut.

After a few minutes of the faithful conversation that would be keeping Shubman awake tonight, Ishan was called by Rohit for something. Abhishek instantly turned to Shubman and whispered, "He is scary." And then pretended to shiver. 'God, he is just as dramatic, isn't he?' Shubman thought before shaking his head adoringly.

Abhishek then started sharing few of the interesting incidents in his life, and some of them had Shubman in them too. Though he didn't remember any of them, he still listened to them just as enthusiastically. After some time, Rishabh joined in on the conversation, and suddenly both of them shifted to bullying Shubman.

There was some commotion heard at the door, and soon a group of people could be seen entering the house. They seemed to be a family. The man was having a conversation with Virat, and the lady came to greet Anushka. "Who are they?" Shubman turned to Rishabh. "Oh, they are some close friends of Virat Bhai. It seems like they were in town, so Virat Bhai invited them too." "oohkay."

The younger of the two men, who looked just a year older than Shubman, came towards them. "Hi Shubman, how are you?" He asked with a smile on his face. "I am fine, thank you." "I am Karan, by the way; you might not remember me, but we have met before." He said, extending his hand towards Shubman, which Shubman took gratefully.

This Karan guy seemed like a decent person, and Shubman was happy to be meeting new people. "hello. Sorry about my memories." "no no. Please don't worry about it. We were not too familiar anyway. Just get well soon, okay?" And he left with that. "He is better than his sister," Abhishek said after he had left. He received a side eye from Rishabh at that.

"Why?" Shubman asked them. He felt like there was something wrong, and he didn't know what it was. "His sister is too full of herself; that's it." "You just don't like her, Abhi." "Nope, Rishabh bhai, she is too self-obsessed." "She has always been decent in front of us. Spoke politely, and all. I don't know what problem you have with her." "Maybe you just don't like her, Abhi," Shubman added helpfully.

"Ugh, here she comes," Abhishek said, looking straight ahead. A pretty girl wearing a simple black dress that seemed elegant and not too revealing could be seen walking towards them. She looked good, and the aura around her seemed to be that of some celebrity. She walked towards them and extended her hand towards Shubman for a handshake.

"Hello Shubi, How are you doing?" Shubman took the hand in a firm and professional handshake and passed her a small smile. "I am fine." "oh right. I am Meera." She smiled again, and Shubman instantly became awkward. She then started a conversation with Rishabh, and Shubman slowly excused himself from the conversation.

"She doesn't seem that bad," Shubman said once he was near Abhishek again and away from Meera and Rishabh, to which Abhishek just rolled his eyes and went on to refill his glass, leaving Shubman to himself again. Soon Rishabh was done with whatever conversation he was having with Meera, and he returned to his original position beside Shubman.

"What is the dynamic between her and Abhi?" Shubman asked Rishabh once he returned. "Nothing. He is just a hater." "sounds about right." And they both shared a laugh at that. 'Abhishek really could be too much sometimes.' Thought shubman.

Shubman's eyes, just like in the car, were returning to Ishan here too. He was staring too much, he realized when he made eye contact with Rohit for the third time in a row, who was currently having a conversation with Ishan. Rohit smirked at him, took his drink, said something to Ishan, and went away.

Ishan looked at Shubman, and Shubman felt like going back into the coma was the best option right now because the heartache he felt when Ishan looked back at him with that pretty smile of his was not natural at all. They were still looking at each other when Shubman saw Meera approaching Ishan with a smile he didn't like.

Ishan turned to her when she told him something, and soon they got engaged in a conversation. Meera's body language, Shubman realized, had changed completely from when she was talking with him. Now her hands didn't seem to have a control over themselves and were all over Ishan, which to Shubman's distaste, Ishan didn't mind at all.

Her eyes were flirty and her smile stupid. Shubman couldn't see Ishan's facial expressions, but that was good because he didn't want to see them either. Shubman had frowned his eyebrows now, and he didn't even try to hide the fact that he was staring at them anymore. Then he made eye contact with Meera, and as if burned, he instantly looked away. Making an eye contact with Rishabh now, who was also staring at him, smirking.

"Earth to shubman." He said, waving his hands in front of Shubman's face. "What?" Shubman asked because the eyes with which Rishabh was staring at him were starting to weigh heavy over him. "Nothing. Wanna ask something?" "What is going on between them?" Shubman asked as if on cue, gesturing towards where Ishan and Meera stood talking to each other.

Suddenly Rishabh and Abhishek, who Shubman didn't realize was standing beside him for quite some time now, started to laugh their heads off. "What's wrong?" Shubman asked, frustrated. "Nothing. Hahahah, this seems like déjà vu to me. Hahhhahaa." Abhishek replied, barely containing his laughter.

"déjà vu?" shubman questioned.  "Why are you asking that shubman? Are you jealous?" Rishabh asked, lifting one of his eyebrows. "jealous? What? NO. Why would I even be jealous? That's just ridiculous." "Righttt. You are definitely not jealous, hahaha," Abhishek interrupted again.

"Shut up, Abhi. Let the boy breathe." Rishabh scolded Abhishek that didn't sound genuine at all. Shubman just squinted his eyes at them. "okay. Okay, she is Meera, the daughter of one of the businessmen Virat Bhai knows and is close to. She usually attends all of the parties Virat Bhai throws. And to answer your question, no, there is nothing going on between her and Ishan. She has a huge crush on him, that's it." Rishabh completed with a smile.

"And what about him?" Shubman finally asked the question that was weighing down upon him. "Ishan is quite literally blind when it comes to people who have a crush on him. Apart from him, the whole world would know that the person has a crush on him. But as far as I know, Ishan has no such feelings for her." Rishabh passed a smile to Abhishek at that.

Shubman after that incident couldn't help but think about why they felt that he was jealous. Why would he be jealous? Of whom? That was just stupid. 'Yes, that's right; they were just being stupid.' Shubman thought and looked at Ishan one more time, but this time Ishan was already looking at him. He smiled when their eyes met, and Shubman's heart skipped a beat. Shubman left the room with the thought that he was definitely getting sick and needed fresh air.

Title: He Is.....Pretty?
Chapter_Text:
Shubman, after leaving the main party area, found himself in a secluded corner of the room with just a few people nearby, busy with some topic they were discussing. The loud music of the party wasn't that ear-damaging here, and one didn't need to shout at the top of their voice to let the other person hear them.

Don't get him wrong; Shubman loved music and parties, but maybe this was all too much for him. The environment and the people were all a bit too much knowledge dumped on his forgetful brain. Or perhaps he was feeling too suffocated inside of that room?

Shubman was busy thinking of why he came here, leaving all the people behind. Now that he thinks about it, he didn't even inform anyone that he was going to step out for a second. How unethical and stupid of him. Shubman, if sent in an overthinking and self-sabotaging competition, would win the first prize. That was not even an overstatement.

He liked to torment his brain and think about stupid stuff that would most definitely never happen, but he still liked to believe that they would and he would die because of some words that he might have said somewhere. While thinking about something similar, Shubman went towards a balcony. He sat there admiring the night.

Shubman has always been a night person, but he had never really sat and admired the night like this before. Now that he was doing it, he realized just how beautiful it all looked. The dark sky breathing and vast, the stars stuck on it like some décor. It seemed as if the creator thought that the black sky would look too depressing without some shine and added that shine in the form of stars.

It looked very beautiful. Shubman had never really seen something so simple yet so breathtakingly beautiful before. In the age of people being a mountain or sea person, he would rather be a mere spectator witnessing this beauty of the night. So serene yet so impactful. Full of mystery, yearning to be studied.

Just as he was going on to admire the beauty of the moon in that suddenly overly philosophical mind of his, he heard a voice from behind him, "Shubi?" Shubman's heart skipped a beat yet again. No matter how much he thought about it, he just couldn't think of why his heart would do that, and so he decided to let go of the topic for the time being.

"Why are you out here? People were all looking for you inside." Ishan continued when he didn't get any reply from Shubman. "Just came for some fresh air." "Okay." Ishan went inside and got himself a chair, settling right beside Shubman. Shubman, who noticed all of it, didn't know how to react anymore, so he just smiled at Ishan and went back to looking at the sky again.

Now that Shubman had Ishan right beside him looking at the sky with him, he lost all interest in the night sky. Now, Shubman had something prettier to admire. 'what? What the fuck am I even thinking?' Shubman forced the thought of Ishan's beauty out of his head, and it took all the willpower inside of him to keep his eyes on the stars and not on the moon beside him. 'There is no way I am comparing him with the moon right now? Have I completely lost my mind?'

His terrain of thoughts was interrupted when he heard a throaty laugh beside his ear. He finally threw his sanity out the window and took a look at Ishan laughing; his eyes were closed and his head was thrown back. He held the arm of the chair to prevent himself from falling, his neck on full display.

Shubman had his eyes stuck at the mole on his neck for some reason. He had never been some romantic fool, but hell, was that mole pretty? 'Alright, I am definitely drunk because what the actual fuck?' Shubman wanted to slap himself so badly right now, but he was stopped yet again when Ishan finally decided to speak.

"Why are you glaring at the sky like that? What did it even do to you?" Shubman was left speechless at that. Was he really glaring? "Why are you making that face shubi?" "nothing. I was just thinking about something." "Yeah, right, I could see that, but what is it that you are thinking about so seriously"

Shubman just smiled, thinking 'you' and then immediately internally slapped himself at the thought. "Shubman, really, are you okay?" Ishan now looked quite concerned for him, and Shubman didn't know if he should laugh or cry at that. "Ouch," Shubman uttered when someone slapped his head from behind. "What is it, Rishabh?" he asked Rishabh, who had been behind that slap on his head, his tone irritated.

"Stop staring at Ishan and come inside; everyone is asking for you." Rishabh pulled Shubman out of his seat at that, resulting in him losing his footing and falling over Ishan's seat. He landed his hands over the arms of Ishan's chair with little to no distance between both their faces.

Shubman realized that he had never seen eyes as pretty as Ishan's ever. Ishan had the most beautiful pair of eyes. These eyes were wide open in surprise and were looking at Shubman through the lashes so long and black that it reminded him of the night sky again. There was a pretty frown on Ishan's forehead, and Shubman wanted to massage it gently.

"SHUBMANNN," Rishabh shouted in his ears so loud that he thought that he was going to lose his hearing the next moment. Shubman was instantly startled awake from his fantasies and weird thoughts. His hands instinctively went to massage his eardrums and give Rishabh a nasty look.

"What are you shouting like that for? Do you want me to go deaf or what?" His irritated voice was directed at Rishabh, and Rishabh just looked at him like he had grown two heads. "what?" "nothing. Let's go." Rishabh pulled Shuman towards the lively atmosphere of the party again, and they both exited from the quiet.

Ishan was still sitting in that exact position and slouched a bit. He looked at the retreating figures of Rishabh and Shubman, a breath held in his throat. Ishan breathed out, not knowing why his breath got stuck like that.

Rishabh had dragged Shubman to a corner of the lively party room. "Why did you drag me here?" Shubman questioned, looking quite pissed. "Wow, Shubi, I really don't know whether I should be proud of you or should I be concerned. How are you acting in the exact same way you did before losing your memories? Like an idiot." "what? how?" Shubman questioned, confused by what Rishabh said. "Nothing." Rishabh just left with that.

Now that Shubman thought about it, Rishabh did look quite concerned. But why? While he was thinking hard about this weird reaction of Rishabh, he saw him coming towards him again, but this time with a few people at his heels. "Shubman, they wanted to meet you before leaving." A few cricketers were coming towards him.

Shubman smiled and greeted them all. They all wished for him to get better and play together with them once again. Hardik just smirked at him before saying something to Rishabh, and then they both shared a laugh, leaving Shubman speechless at that. Soon Abhishek joined him and Rishabh on the sofa.

"Where were you?" "Busy glaring at the sky." Ishan came from behind them, answering Abhishek. "Glaring the sky? What new hobby is that, Shubi?" Rishabh said that with such a weird smile that Shubman felt chills run through his body. And it might have shown on his face because both Rishabh and Abhishek shared a laugh.

"We should leave too; I promised your mother to drop you off before too late at night," Ishan told Shubman. Abhishek and Rishabh were hooting at the back for some reason neither Ishan nor Shubman knew of. And so, they decided to ignore them and go greet Virat instead.

Shubman met a couple of cricketers before he found himself in the car with Ishan again. This time he had made up his mind to just stare out of the window and not at Ishan. The ride back to Shubman's place was quiet, with only the occasional sounds of the passing vehicles. No one said anything, but it was not an awkward silence. It was quite comfortable, actually.

Soon they reached Shubman's place, and Shubman got out of the car. His sister might have seen them return or heard the car pulling up because she immediately came to open the door for Shubman. "Shubii, you returned? You didn't drink, did you?" was the first thing she asked him when she reached the car.

"No, di, he didn't," Ishan answered her instead. "Knew I could trust you, cutie." And that was directed at Ishan, to which Shubman just gave his sister a side eye, and Ishan let out a laugh. "Alright then, I'll take my leave. Bye, di. Bye, Shubi." "what? Stay the night, Ishan; it's too late." Shahneel spoke before Shubman could, and he completely agreed with his sister.

"No, di, I'd rather go. I have morning practice tomorrow. The coach will kill me if I miss it." shahneel looked like she wanted to press Ishan to stay some more, but then she decided against it. Suddenly, Shubman said, "Bye, Ishan, drive safe." It came out too soft; even Shubman was startled at that.

"Bye," Ishan said with a smile and shook his head at Shubman adoringly before driving off into the night. Shubman still had his eyes widened from the shock, and shahneel looked like her stomach ached due to holding the laugh for too long. "Come inside now, Shubi; stop standing there like a damn statue."

Shahneel dragged Shubman inside the house with that. He just let himself get dragged because why would he find 101 ways to get himself embarrassed always?

Title: Improvement
Chapter_Text:
Shubman had recently started watching the cricket matches he had missed over the years. Coincidentally, all of the matches that he watched somehow had Ishan in them. He totally didn't search for those specific ones to watch at all.

Shubman had sat through the night, surfing through the early matches of Ishan's career. He was an impeccable player, Shubman realized. His playing style, stance, and everything on the whole were admirable. But Shubman wasn't just watching these matches for Ishan now, was he?

He was watching it to learn more about his co-players and to learn more about their cricketing careers. At least this is what he told his sister when she asked about this new hobby of his. She made a weird face but didn't question it any further. The doctors and physiotherapists had reduced their scheduled checkups from daily to every two days and now once a week.

Things were going brilliantly for Shubman, and everyone was quite happy with it. Ishan didn't visit Shubman as much as he used to because now there was an upcoming tournament that he had to practice for. He got busier with these practices as the days passed. But Ishan religiously called Shubman. Everyday.

No matter at what time, there wasn't a day that Ishan didn't call. Their conversations earlier limited to cricket and Shubman's health improvement were now open to mental health and personal lives also. Shubman realized that he loved talking to Ishan. Maybe a bit too much. To the point that he didn't miss any of his calls.

Shubman had stopped sleeping at weird times just so he wouldn't accidentally miss one of Ishan's calls. He knew that if he missed the call, it would be tough for Ishan to call him again; he was busy after all. And it warmed his heart to know that despite being so busy, Ishan never once forgot calling him.

Shubman in the times when he didn't have to do his routine exercises watched cricket matches and made notes. Don't get him wrong, he did watch other people play too, but he got this idea of watching the now-old matches because he wanted to see Ishan playing. So, he watched one match, then he watched two matches, and now it has given way to a series of matches watched and numerous players analyzed.

Now, Shubman had a brief knowledge of almost all the players on the team with him and a sea of knowledge about Ishan Kishan. Somebody can wake him up in the middle of the night and ask about the cricketing career of Ishan Kishan, and Shubman would still say it all correctly. It was all itching at the back of his brain; he didn't know that it was there. Now, after watching all these matches, these memories were unlocking themselves.

Sometimes he would search for some specific match because his conscious told him that it was an amazing match, and when watched, it would indeed be an amazing match. Sometimes, he would randomly remember some incident that didn't happen in the recent past of his broken memories and spend hours thinking more about that incident.

Shubman didn't dislike this outburst of random information at random times, but he wasn't a fan of it either, because then he would spend the majority of his time thinking about it, and it honestly gave him a headache almost always.

He told about it to his doctors of course, and they said that it was completely normal and asked Shubman to refrain from preying too much about the details of these scenarios. Sometimes, when done with all the thinking, he would ask about them to Ishan, and Ishan, like the good friend he was, almost always knew the details of these scenarios.

Shubman loved listening about his life from Ishan's point of view. He didn't know why, but he did like it. These conversations were fun when they lasted, but Shubman would feel the air shifting a bit at the end of all of these conversations, it wasn't like he didn't ask Ishan about it, but Ishan would just change the topic, and then they would resume talking like nothing happened.

This week couldn't be going any better for Shubman because he got a call from BCCI today asking him to join the upcoming tournament as a spectator. Shubman would travel with the team and stay with the team; he wouldn't play, of course. He wasn't fit enough to play yet, but he would get to be on the field watching them play and learning from them.

To Shubman, this was an amazing opportunity because this was a huge step in regaining his career in cricket. He was touched to hear that they were bothered enough to let him in. "Try talking with all of them and try regaining your memories, Shubman; this is the best opportunity to do that." Shahneel had told him after she heard about the news.

Shubman had nodded positive and went to sleep. They were to be deported to England for the tour the day after tomorrow, and Shubman was ecstatic, to say the least. The night before the flight, he couldn't sleep until late at night, but he woke up before his alarm and got out of bed to get ready.

His mother had packed his bag for him, and he felt he hadn't aged a day, making his heart go all mushy. The team bus was to come to pick him up, and he wasn't used to being spoilt like that, even if he was the younger child. So, he got up and got ready because he didn't want to spoil whatever reputation he might have made because he got late.

His sister teased him to no end, and Shubman remembered the day he had first gotten selected for a major tournament ten years ago, and his sister had the same reaction. He didn't need to have his memories to know her response every time he had something major going on. He smiled at her despite her teasing, because where else would he get a sister like her?

Shubman jumped up the bus the moment it got there. He was greeted by familiar faces, and he felt peaceful being around them. The crowd was cheering for him when he got on the bus and pulled him in for a hug. After the hug broke, someone dragged him to a seat. When he looked at who the person was, he was pleasantly (not so) surprised to see that it was Ishan.

He sat beside him, and no one batted an eye. They reached the airport, and Ishan sat there beside him, and then they boarded the plane, and Ishan sat there beside him too. As if it were the most normal thing to do. It came naturally to them. 'He is your best friend; of course, you'll stick together everywhere.' His mind supplied.

But nothing was helpful when Ishan dropped his head on Shubman's shoulder after he had dozed off. Shubman short-circuited. He had become so stiff that Rishabh, who was sitting just a seat ahead of him, took notice of it and started laughing until his head fell off. Luckily for Shubman, most of the members were sleeping, but those who were awake were tormenting him enough to last a lifetime.

After some time, he had relaxed, and soon he slept too. His sleep was half broken due to some commotion near their seat, and before he could open his eyes and see what was happening, there were suddenly hands placed on both of his ears, as if wanting to block off the noise. And it did work because now the background was all muffled.

But Ishan's voice came clear to Shubman as if speaking right next to his ear, "Can you go do this somewhere else? Shubman is sleeping." "When he sleeps beside you, Ishan, even a nuclear bomb fails to wake him up." Someone said, "Oh, please, Rishabh, fuck off. I swear if he wakes up due to your stupidity, I will personally plant lizards in your luggage."

"Do you think I am scared of that?" "You want me to prove my theory?" and that seemed to have made Rishabh retreat. Shubman didn't want to smile physically because then Ishan would know that he wasn't sleeping, but he smiled internally before dozing off again.

The second time Shubman woke up was because someone shook his shoulders so hard that he thought that he would dislocate them if this continued for a minute more, so he decided to wake up. He realized that it was Hardik who was trying to wake him up. "Finally, Shubman, I thought you were dead." Shubman gave him a look and then decided to look out the window instead.

"Let's go; we are here. Or do you want to be ported somewhere else?" Rishabh said, pulling him out of his seat. Shubman begrudgingly took his backpack and exited the plane with the team. The departure from the airport to the hotel took them an hour, and now they were all in the lobby of the hotel they had booked.

"Alright people, we have fewer rooms, so, two for one, come on, choose your roommates," Rohit said after they talked to the receptionist. "Ishan and Shubman. Your keys." Virat handed Ishan a key, and Shubman looked baffled at that. He looked like he wanted to ask something but decided against it.

"Why do you look like that? You always share rooms with him." Rishabh said, looking at Shubman, which made a few other heads turn too. "You wanted to stay with someone else, Shubi?" Ishan asked him, and the "no." came out of him instantly. Rishabh, like the bitchhead he was, laughed at that. And when Shubman looked around, almost everyone was trying to control their smile.

"Come on, Shubman, you stay with him even if they had you allotted a separate room of your own. It's almost as if you are glued to him." Virat said teasingly, and Shubman raised his eyebrows at that. "Wait, you can share the room with me for a change. Ugh, Rohit bhai, why did you have to give them the keys so fast? We could have actually made Shubman stay away from Ishan. It would have been an opportunity of a lifetime, bhaiya."

And everyone looked like they were all as regretful as Rishabh was. "In your dreams, Rishabh Pant, now give us a side, please; I want to go to sleep. I don't have enough energy to spend on your nonsense." Ishan said, pushing Rishabh to the side, grabbing Shubman's hand, and then proceeding to hunt for their room.

Everyone, including Rishabh, just looked at the retreating figure of Ishan with Shubman at his heels, with an adoring smile on their faces. "Come on, decide your rooms." Hardik clapped twice to gain the attention of the crowd before speaking. They all then went back to deciding rooms for themselves with a smile on their faces.

Title: Jealous? Yes.
Chapter_Text:
Shubman, after reaching the room, sprawled on the bed instantly. "At least change Shubman." Ishan's said, who had just exited the washroom, hair still dripping wet and a towel in hand. "I am sleepy." Replied Shubman, who now faced the window on the other side of the room, opposite of where Ishan stood.

The wet hair and the clothes sticking to his body due to the moisture had Shubman's brain freeze. He tried his best to avoid looking at him and faced the other way. What Shubman couldn't understand was why Ishan had such an effect on him. Or maybe he had just completely lost his mind now.

The bed dipped at the foot, and Shubman instantly sprang up, took his clothes, and banged the washroom close. Ishan was left on the bed still with a towel, staring behind the now-closed washroom, thinking what the fuck was wrong with Shubman. It wasn't new for them to be sharing rooms, but this stupidity of Shubman was new for him indeed.

Ishan, like a good friend, waited for Shubman to get done so that both of them could rest a bit, but Shubman took his sweet time in the bathroom. After what felt like centuries, Shubman finally got out of the washroom with dripping hair and body-fitting clothes that seemed to be wet too.

"what....?" Ishan started before realizing just what was wrong... "You forgot to take the towel?" asked a very stunned Ishan. "Shubman, are you stupid? There was one inside too, if you just opened your eyes, Shubi, and you could have always asked me for one, you know." Shubman looked very embarrassed at that. "I didn't want to bother you."

Ishan got up from where he was sitting on the bed and immediately put his palm against Shubman's forehead. "Are you sick?" "No, I am okay." Said Shubman, taking Ishan's hand off of his forehead. "Dry your hair at least," Ishan said while grabbing a towel to dry Shubman's hair for him.

They were right in front of the mirror, and Shubman's eyes were fixated on Ishan's face. "There is something you want to say?" Ishan asked, meeting eyes with him. Shubman looked away, breaking the eye contact, and shook his head in negative. "You sure you aren't sick?" "No, Ishan, I am fine; I just need sleep." "Right, it has been so long since you last travelled; it must be hard. Alright, I am almost done with your hair; you can sleep."

Shubman got up from the chair and went to the bed to lie down, immediately closing his eyes. Ishan, on the other hand, switched the lights off, closed the curtains, and tucked the blanket over Shubman before lying beside him and dozing off, too.

Shubman didn't sleep a wink before it was time for them to get off to eat dinner. He just lay there awake until he got a message from Virat to come downstairs. He was tired—too tired to even lift a finger—but he just couldn't sleep. It hadn't happened to him before, and he couldn't figure out what was wrong. So, he decided to get up and freshen up before waking Ishan too and going to get the dinner.

"Ishan, the dinner is ready. Wake up." Said Shubman, shaking Ishan a bit. He, on the other hand, just changed his position and continued sleeping again. "ishaann." He said it a bit louder this time. Ishan stirred a bit before opening his eyes to see Shubman too close to his face, so as a reflex, he kicked Shubman off the bed.

"What?" Shubman was left on the floor speechless. "I am so sorry, Shubi. I am up. Let's go." He said helping Shubman up from the floor. "You didn't have to kick me like that." "I am sorry, okay," Ishan said before going off to freshen up, leaving Shubman to his element once again.

Later, when they were both coming downstairs to eat with the team, Shubman instantly tensed up. There, sitting right beside Virat, was Meera. He couldn't wrap his head around why this girl would join them at their team dinner. He went towards the table and sat right across from her beside Rishabh, who was looking at him with mischievous eyes.

Ishan, who came down with him, was busy chatting with some staff members before he noticed that Shubman had already sat at the table, so he went there too. Meera, who saw Ishan coming towards the table, stood to grab a chair for him, but she was just halfway up when Ishan came and took a seat beside Shubman.

This didn't escape the eyes of anyone on the table, and they all gave each other a look before continuing what they were doing. Shubman, on the other hand, was shocked and speechless. He just kept looking at Ishan. "Ishan, all the best for the match." Meera finally said when the air was shifted to lively again. "Thank you.... Oh hi Meera I didn't see you there. When did you get here?"

"Just came to cheer you up; Virat bhai insisted that I stay for dinner." "Of course, he did. You are like a sister to all of us, right, Ishan?" Rishabh asked, and Shubman for the first time felt like he could say that he liked Rishabh. "absolutely." Ishan replied while serving himself and Shubman some food.

"Bhai? Isn't this too oily for Shubman?" Ishan said, looking at Hardik, who was the one to order the food. "Is Shubman a baby?" Meera said, interrupting again. To her, it was a joke because she was smiling while she said it, but the tone with which she said it, wasn't friendly at all. It had bitterness in it.

She got looks from all across the table, but they all decided to ignore her. "I mean, we have people younger than him on the team; why is he babied like that? He can eat the oil, you know. Won't die, trust me." She smiled at Shubman while taking a bite of the food. Her words had hidden venom in them.

She sounded like she had been waiting to say all of this. Shubman was stunned speechless. He couldn't understand why he was the target of her biting words suddenly. To him, she didn't sound friendly at all, not even remotely. Shubman was still busy thinking about what he did wrong when he heard from beside him.

"Have you gone insane?" Ishan's words were cold, and he never spoke like that to anyone. It shocked everyone on the table, not because someone was saying something like that but because was Ishan saying it. Rishabh, who was just about to reply to her nasty words, just looked at Ishan and smiled. "I was just joking, Ishan." Meera tried to reason with him, but he just scoffed at her before talking to the waiter.

Nobody dared to cut Ishan's words because this Ishan was scary. "I am really sorry, Ishan. I didn't mean it like that." "You don't have to be sorry, Meera. I know what you meant and what you didn't, so I would suggest that you continue eating before I lose my cool." This seemed to have pushed the wrong buttons because Meera instantly flipped.

"He can do anything, and nobody blinks an eye, and just when I say the truth, you start losing your cool, Ishan? just amazing."  "What in the world did he say to you?" Hardik asked. He didn't like the way she spoke to Ishan at all. "You don't know?" Her words were mocking, and Shubman didn't like the fact that he didn't understand what he did wrong.

"Truth is Meera, you like Ishan, and he doesn't like you; that's it. You can just accept it and go home. There is no need to channel your anger on other people." Rishabh's words seemed to have jabbed her in the right places. Because she looked like she just got stung. "I don't stay where I am not respected." With that, she just took her bag and left the hotel.

They were all quiet for some time, looking at each other. And then they all suddenly burst into laughter, "What is wrong with you?" Virat said while laughing. "Your good friend's daughter just went away angry, bhai, what now?" Ishan was too good sometimes. "My good friend doesn't like her all that much either." Rishabh was the one to laugh the loudest at that.

"Don't mind her too much; people do insane things when they are in love." "We know Yash; she isn't a bad person," Rohit replied to Yashasvi, smiling at him. "I shouldn't have talked to her like that. She has always been sweet to me." Ishan said he regretted his earlier outburst. "Of course, she has Ishan; she likes you."

"what? I just thought you were saying that to piss her off. Does she actually like me?" "Yeah, I mean, if it wasn't obvious enough, yes, she does." Hardik finally lost his cool with Ishan and snapped at him. "Why have I never noticed that?" "Because Ishan, you are blind when it comes to people who like you. So many people have a crush on you and you don't even know; you are the most oblivious person I know. Not even kidding."

Rishabh sounded like he was done with Ishan and his antics now. Shubman was in his world while they were all busy fighting amongst themselves. He had a lot to think about. He knew that Meera liked Ishan, but what problem did she have with him? If they were just friends, then why would she target him of all people?

'Maybe he liked... No what nonsense.' Shubman was having a complete monologue with himself. He didn't like it when Ishan talked about Meera so softly; he didn't like the fact that he was regretful about snapping at her earlier. Was he jealous? But that was impossible, right?

Shubman was brought back from his turmoil when Rishabh elbowed him. "What are you thinking about?" "Poor guy is stunned," Virat added helpfully. "You didn't have to listen to her, you know. Should have snapped right back at her. Why did you sit there listening to her nonsense?" Ishan, although regretful, still looked a bit angry.

"Well, you did it for him, so it's okay now." Hardik casually said before going back to eating again. Rishabh was still smirking at Shubman, and he had no idea why. So, ignoring him, Shubman went back to his food too.

Title: Do I Like Him?
Chapter_Text:
Shubman's eyes kept looking for Ishan ever since that incident at the dinner. He was freaking out because now this didn't feel right anymore. At first, he thought that it kept happening because Ishan seemed like such an amazing friend, and he wanted to know more about him, know about everything that they talked about, and everything that he knew about Ishan before losing his memories.

Now though, he doesn't want to know more about Ishan just because he is his best friend. He wants to drink in every habit of his. He wants to know about the way Ishan's sleep cycles work, what he likes to eat, and where he likes to go on vacation. Who he is closest to in the family and what he likes to do in his free time.

Shubman wants to know about Ishan's past and present in a very detailed manner. Such that it should include every detail in a very accurate manner. He wants to know Ishan better than even Ishan knows himself. And all of these thoughts have now become too scary for Shubman.

Shubman is scared of his own thoughts. It is definitely not normal to have this urge to know a person to that extent. Shubman is afraid to even look at Ishan anymore. But his eyes, they had this nick to always betray him. And now, like a madman, Shubman was having a beef with his own eyes. How amazing.

After Shubman finished his dinner, he went straight to his and Ishan's shared room without sparing anyone a glance. Ishan and the others at the table were left speechless at this behavior of his. Usually, after they eat together there is always an after-session of stupidity, but Shubman didn't indulge himself in this luxury at all.

He didn't even wait for Ishan to finish his food so that they could go back together. "Did Meera manage to get on the remaining of his brain cells?" Rishabh asked no one in particular but received looks from all around the table. Some seemed to have nodded along with Rishabh, some were still stunned, and some (just Ishan) gave him a nasty look.

Shubman, when he reached the room, flopped himself on the bed instantly. Without a single thought in mind (thoughts of Ishan took all the space), he fell asleep due to exhaustion. Earlier he hadn't slept a wink, and now he was too tired to stay awake anymore. Later at night, Shubman, still in deep sleep, saw a blurry vision of an accident, and it looked horrendous.

He was stunned awake due to the intensity of this dream of his. When he woke up, he found himself in a proper sleeping position that didn't compromise his neckbone, with a blanket draped over him, which he had no memory of using earlier. The lights weren't on anymore, but he could still feel a calm figure sleeping beside him.

So, Shubman got up and went out of the room onto the balcony without creating any disturbance. He sat there in the chilly night with a glass of water in hand, enjoying the moment's peace. He was content, but there was this increased turmoil in his heart, the reason for which he didn't seem to be able to pinpoint yet.
