He wanted to use this time to think about ways he could make his time more meaningful and how he could adjust to this new memory mashup of his, but instead, all he did was think about Ishan. His heart couldn't comprehend the existence of someone so beautiful yet handsome at the same time.

Earlier at the dining table, no matter how much he tried, he wasn't successful in taking his eyes off of Ishan and his pretty eyes that seemed to have held the galaxies in them, that acne under his left eye, which made him look even more majestic than he already was, and oh, the mole on his neck.

Shubman had a different kind of obsession with that mole; every time it caught his eye, it took over his mind completely, so much so that he couldn't think about anything else anymore. It was unhealthy but so addictive, and Shubman was becoming an addict. 'Fuck Shubman, get a fucking grip.' He scolded himself before watching the lights in the distance.

The nightlight in the far distance reminded him of the light in Ishan's eyes yet again, and Shubman thought that he had officially gone insane because what the actual fuck was he even thinking? He desperately needed a break from this whole Ishan topic that went on and on inside of his head 24/7 like a broken record.

So, in the hopes of breaking free from this constant brain rot, Shubman decided to open Instagram to find solace in his fucked-up feed. But to his disappointment (really?) all he saw on his for you page was cricket, specifically, Ishan. It was all Ishan.

There were videos that the fans had edited of him playing cricket, his interviews, brand collaborations, parties he had attended, and whatnot. And honestly, Ishan looked too good in these edits. 'Talented toh hai ye fans, I mean, won't give too much credit to them either because, with a face like Ishan's, it would be hard to not look good now, wouldn't it?' Shubman thought, smiling to himself.

Yeah, he looked stupid, and if anyone were to walk in on him at this moment, they would definitely be very concerned for his mental health, but he couldn't care less. Scrolling through his feed, he suddenly entered something that made him even more confused than he already was.

#Ishman. There were reels with this hashtag, and they all had videos of him and Ishan in them. He had never really searched about himself on the internet before; it was very awkward watching himself at first, but then he got a hold of it. While watching these reels, Shubman realized two things.

The first one was that people shipped them. They shipped Ishan and him as couples, and his heart skipped a few beats at the thought. They all went on about how Shubman looked at Ishan and stuff, and here comes the second thing he realized. He realized they might not be completely wrong.

Because watching these videos, there is no way he wasn't interested in Ishan in a way he shouldn't have been. There was definitely something in his eyes. Shubman can be an airhead sometimes, but this was so obvious that he couldn't even gaslight himself into believing that nothing was going on.

'Okay, wait a minute.' Now, when Shubman thought back about it, all the smirks from all the people around him made more sense to him than they did before. 'So, was I always in love? Have I fallen now? Were we just friends before? Did I like him? Do I like him now?' There were so many questions that were left unanswered, and it made Shubman's head ache.

'Fucking hell, why are my eyes glued to him like that? And why am I smiling like that? Is it just camera work? Am I thinking too much into it? Do I think like that because I feel like I like him now? I wasn't in love with him since before, was I?' Shubman reluctantly shut his phone off because the headaches were starting again.

Shubman still in the thoughts of ishan and his relationship before he lost his memories dozed off where he was sitting earlier. He was unsuccessful in sorting his feelings out but at least he got to complete his sleep peacefully.

"shubmannnn..." Shubman could hear someone calling out to him from a distance, but he couldn't see anything beyond the darkness. "SHUBMAN." This time the voice was loud and clear, and he was jerked awake. Looking around, he found himself in the same position on the balcony as he had been last night.

Now the sun was shining brightly in the sky, and its rays made it hard for Shubman to open his eyes properly and look around to add 2 and 2. "Are you insane? Why did you sleep here? It's so cold out here; have you gone mad?" It was Ishan losing his mind, Shubman realized. He just kept staring at him like he had lost his ability to understand what Ishan was trying to say.

"Come inside the room right now, Shubman Gill." This time his voice was too firm to not oblige to, so Shubman stumbled up from where he was sleeping earlier and wobbled his way to the bed. "See, your legs are asleep; why would you even sleep like that?" "Sorry, I just woke up last night and went out for some fresh air and accidently fell asleep." Shubman sheepishly explained.

"Get ready now; we need to go down for breakfast and then for practice," Ishan said while going inside the bathroom with his towel. And Shubman slept again for the time being. "Shubman, are you serious? Uth ja warna bohot maar khaega." Ishan could be heard shouting yet again.

He came out of the bathroom after a good 30 minutes and was trying to wake shubman up since he had re-entered the room. Shubman on the other hand kept sleeping on with out paying any heed to ishan and his struggles of waking him up.

After a lot of struggles, Ishan was successful in sending Shubman to freshen up before he got himself busy with something random. Shubman, despite the sleepiness, couldn't shake off the thoughts from last night. He was still struggling with the weight of all these questions he couldn't find the answers to.

(Apologies for the delayed update. I will do my best to keep you updated, so please bear with me. Thank you very much for your patience.)

Title: Have I Always Liked You?
Chapter_Text:
Shubman, with the newly found information about the possibility of having a crush on his own best friend, felt this weird sense of contentment. He knew that this was wrong and that it wouldn't be reciprocated ever, but the thought of this crush, even though one-sided, made him smile to himself more often than he'd like to accept.

"Shubman bhai, pagal toh nahi hogaya hai?" Siraj commented during the breakfast. Rishabh, noticing the way Shuman had suddenly turned deaf, decided to join in the teasing too: "Usko mood swings hore hai; yesterday he was too mad to sit with us a bit longer aur aaj hamare beech sirf sharir hai uska, atma toh stroll pe nikli hai."

This sentence brought the current state of Shubman's wandering consciousness into the limelight. "Shubman....shubman." Virat tried getting his attention, but it was all fruitless. "SHUBMAN." Rohit had finally lost his last straw of patience; really, he wasn't paid enough to deal with this crackhead of a team.

"Haan haan bhai, kya hua?" Shubman was stunned out of his daydreams at that. Now, he looked too innocent to scold. "Kiske khayalo main ghum ho gill?" Ishan asked mischievously, stunning everyone at the table. "It has been so long since I last heard you teasing him, Ishu." Virat said, smiling at Ishan. "bhai. Seriously?" "Mazak Mazak." Virat tried defending himself from Ishan's laser eyes.

"Ek baat puchu?" "Everyone shut up; this is a canon event," Rishabh said more than happy to answer Shubman. "Kya Rishabh bolne de bache ko. Pooch Shubi." Hardik interrupted, trying to save Shubman from Rishabh's teasing.

"Was I in a relationship with someone before losing memories? Or did I like someone?" "Did you come across videos of you and Sara?" Ishan asked very seriously, trying to make sense of Shubman's out-of-the-blue question. "Sara?" Shuman looked and sounded confused.

"Sara Tendulkar?" Ishan supplied trying to be helpful. "Was I in a relationship with her?" Shubman looked quite panicked at that. "No, you weren't. And Ishan, stop confusing the poor man. No, Shubman, you weren't in a relationship with her. You are just shipped with her; people think that you'll are in a relationship, but you'll aren't actually." Hardik provided helpfully.

"But... I have some news: you can be in a relationship with her if you just agree to go on a date with her. That lady would absolutely love you, I promise." Ishan sounded so excited about it that it actually hurt Shubman. "Did I like her?" he asked, genuinely confused. "No, you didn't," Virat casually added.

"To answer you, no, you weren't in a relationship with anyone; although Sara was a PR stunt and you agreed to it, you weren't very keen about going on a date with her, so it was quite clear that you didn't want to date her, and as far as I know, yes, you liked someone; I can't be too sure about it because you never told anyone about it." Rishabh provided helpfully.

"No, he didn't like anyone; what are you even on about?" Ishan said, bringing Rishabh's attention to himself. "Well, he did." Rishabh said, smiling a calm smile. "he did?" Ishan questioned, more to himself than anyone else. "Don't you think I would be the first person he would tell about it if he were to like someone?"

"Well, Ishan, that's the thing; he didn't tell anyone. I just guessed." "Oh." "yeah oh." Laughed Rishabh. "So, you are telling me that I wasn't in a relationship with anyone, but there is a possibility that I liked someone?" "yeah." Shubman looked like he was in deep thought, so everyone decided against disturbing him.

Later, after breakfast, it was time to practice, and the playful banter was shifted to a more serious conversation. Rohit had just spoken with the strategist, and they agreed on certain techniques. Right now, everyone was busy with their own practice, and Shubman had his own coaches who tried to get his stamina and basic practice on track again.

What they made him do was quite similar to what his coaches back home made him do. It was a bit boring for Shubman, but he still tried doing it while putting in his best. 'Is the someone Rishabh mentioned Ishan? Did I like Ishan?' Shubman's thought was brought to Ishan again. Shubman couldn't keep himself from thinking about Ishan for more than 15 minutes.

'What if I liked someone else? I mean, is this feeling towards Ishan just my nerves then? What if I regain my memories? Whom would I like then? Fuckkk, this is actually fucking up my brain.' "Shubman Bhai, are you okay?" Yashasvi brought Shubman's attention away from his inner dilemma, and he looked quite concerned.

"What?" "I have been calling for you for the past 5 minutes. Did you even hear me? Do you need a doctor? Should call Ishan?" "What would Ishan do?" Shubman asked, smiling. "Well, most of your inconveniences just sorts themselves out with the presence of Ishan Bhai, so I thought why not."

And Yashasvi said that with so much innocence that Shubman didn't know how to react anymore, so he just kept looking at him without blinking, scaring the poor boy. "Bhai...?" "Yeah what?" "Are you okay?" "Very well.. why are you here?" "Virat Bhai was asking for you; let's go." He said while guiding Shubman to where Virat was sitting.

"You were asking for me, bhai?" Shubman said as soon as he reached Virat. "Yeah, Shubi, come here and meet the staff." And then Virat started introducing all the staff members to Shubman. He didn't know how to grasp so much information together, but he tried to grasp as much information as possible.

"Try your best and recover as fast as possible; we want to see you play again, Shubman." A sweet-looking woman in her 40's told Shubman. It made his heart go all warm, and he nodded eagerly. "Sushma Aunty is trying to speed Shubman's recovery with sweetness." Ishan came from behind Shubman and commented while smiling, making the lady smile brighter.

Later, Shubman completed his follow-up and was advised to rest for some time. So, he just sat at the bleachers and watched the members practice. He was trying to understand their stance and their gameplay and make the most of this opportunity, but all his brain did was comment on how good Ishan looked while practicing.

'What the fuck, shubman?' He internally scolded himself but couldn't help but wonder if the person he liked was really Ishan. Because there was no way he didn't find this boy charismatic. It was quite literally impossible.

'Should I just ask him?' then he scolded himself again for coming up with such shit ideas. 'Should I ask Shahneel Di? Would she know?' Well, there was only one way to find out, but Shubman was skeptical about it. He was sitting there thinking about what he should do when he saw Ishan walking towards him and wondered what happened. Then he looked again and realized that most of them were walking towards the bleachers.

"You look like you want to ask about something?" Rishabh commented when he reached Shubman. Soon all the other members reached too, and they looked at Shubman after hearing Rishabh. "Well, you know you can ask anything, right?" Ishan provided. "I want to ask something indeed."

"go on." "Well, umhhh, Ishan, well, I wanted to ask you that..." "Can you just ask already? What is up with that awkwardness?" Ishan interrupted, laughing a bit. "Yeah, so, did I always like you?" "What?" Ishan was confused by what Shubman just said, and all the other members were pleasantly surprised.

"Yeah, I mean, I am your best friend. Of course, you liked me always. Or did you not?" Ishan tried to laugh it away, but Shubman and his stupidity had different ideas. "No, no, you got it all wrong. What I mean to ask is have I always had feelings for you, like romantic feelings? Was I romantically into you since before I lost my memories, or have I started losing my mind behind you now?"

"What?" Ishan was too stunned to speak any further, and the bubble popped when Rishabh laughed his heart out. When looked closely, other members were all smiling too; some were teasing, but most of the smiles were happy, sweet smiles. "This shubman is so unhinged, I love it.. hahaha," said Rishabh.

"Yes, Shubi, darling, you always liked him; you just weren't brave enough to tell him that." "What?" Poor Ishan looked like he could faint any moment now. "I was right; the person you said I liked is Ishan, right?" asked Shubman, completely ignoring Ishan's 'What'.

"Yes, it is." Said Rishabh. "Wow, Ishan, you got a person who liked you, lost all his memories about you, and still ended up liking you again," said Virat while smiling at Ishan. "What the actual fuck is going on here?" Ishan had finally lost his last shred of patience.

"It should be quite clear, no? I like you." Shubman went so straightforward that he shocked himself too, along with Ishan. "what?" "Is that the only word you know now?" "I need time to process this. Please excuse me." And Ishan left with that. While the rest just looked at each other with mixed emotions.

Title: Regret?
Chapter_Text:
After the conversation between Ishan and Shubman, a heavy silence enveloped the group; nobody dared to speak, afraid that the fragile illusion would shatter and they would be forced to confront an unwelcomed reality. Shubman hesitated to return to the room he shared with Ishan, realizing in hindsight how foolish his earlier actions had been. To him, Ishan's friendship was relatively new, and he now understood the risk he had taken that could potentially ruin it for good.

To Shubman, Ishan was more of an idea he admired than a fully formed friendship. He couldn't identify the basis of their bond; he hadn't yet fully grasped what Ishan had been through or how he came to have such strong feelings for him. He simply liked Ishan—that was all he knew. Now, as he reflected on it, he recognized that his younger self wasn't a coward; he just didn't want to lose Ishan.

The stories that the old Shubman knew were unknown to the current Shubman. This Shubman had developed feelings for a man and decided it was time to confess. However, the previous Shubman had imagined every scenario of confessing and had lived through all the possibilities of losing Ishan, which had always stopped him from taking that step.

That Shubman was not afraid of liking his best friend, who was also a man; rather, he was terrified of what his feelings could lead to. The thought of losing Ishan alone sent chills down his spine, and the current Shubman would never fully grasp that fear.

The more he pondered, the more his head started to ache. The doctors had advised him to avoid taking in too much information at once or dwelling excessively on the past, but at that moment, he was desperate to learn more about himself, about Ishan, and their friendship. And then everything went blank.

"What's wrong? Why did he faint?" Shubman heard as he regained consciousness. The voice was very familiar, but he couldn't quite place it. "He might have forced himself to think about certain memories he had forgotten, and it was a bit too much for him, resulting in a shutdown. It's okay; he is fine now."

When he opened his eyes, he saw all the team members gathered around the sofa on which he was resting. He tried to smile at them, but then he noticed Ishan standing at the foot of the sofa with the doctor next to him. Ishan was looking at Shubman with a fierce expression that scared him.

After giving him a cold look, Ishan walked away to see the doctor off, leaving Shubman confused and hurt. Rishabh stood beside him, attempting to stifle his laughter, which seemed nearly impossible at that moment. "Bhai, are you okay? You scared everyone," Siraj decided to break the silence. Shubman smiled at him and nodded in response.

"Kya sochne ki koshish kar raha tha? Tu sochta bhi hai? Ye toh nayi information hai," Rishabh said, struggling to suppress his laughter. "Pata nahi bhai, kuch yaad nahi hai," Shubman replied, choosing to engage. Initially, everyone was concerned, but now they all seemed more relaxed.

After a minute or two, Ishan joined them, still glaring at Shubman. He placed two packets of medicine beside him and handed him the medicine from both packets. Without any complaints, Shubman took the medicine and watched as Ishan walked away. Once Ishan was out of sight, Shubman turned his attention back to the others in the room. They appeared to be waiting for Ishan to leave as well.

"I made a mistake, didn't I?" Shubman asked after a while. The group looked a bit disappointed; some even wore tired expressions on their faces. Rishabh was the first to respond. "I think you did nothing wrong. Although the old you would be really furious about this, I still believe you did the right thing." He concluded. "I think you did right too," added Virat, and soon everyone joined in, agreeing with him.

"Don't worry too much about Ishan Bhai; he might be shocked right now.  Although there's no reason for him to be since your feelings for him were clear enough that anyone should have noticed. However, as oblivious as he can be at times, he somehow missed it, and now he's completely taken aback. But don't let that concern you; he'll come around because he can't imagine life without you. Even if he tries, it would be nearly impossible for him to do so." Yashasvi's detailed explanation warmed Shubman's heart.

Shubman simply smiled and decided to save his energy for the inevitable conversation with an angry Ishan later. He hoped to spend the night sleeping here, but his friends insisted he go to his room. When he entered, he was met with complete silence. There was no one in sight, and after a bit of searching, he realized that he was indeed alone in the room.

Ishan was out, and it was quite evident with the vacant washroom, lonely bed, and empty balcony. So, Shubman decided to sleep for some time to acquire enough energy to deal with this mess that he had created himself. Sooner than anticipated, Shubman was already asleep and free from all the useless thinking that he was usually up to when awake.

Ishan was sitting near the bleachers of the stadium, lost in thought as he gazed into the distance. Suddenly, Rishabh approached from behind and said, "There you are! I've been searching for you all over the hotel." He sat down beside Ishan.

"And why are you here, if I may ask?" Ishan replied, turning to look at him. "I wanted to speak with you." "I don't want to talk." "You have to; there's no other choice." "I can just leave, you know?" "No, you can't. Bohot hogaya hai tera, Ishan. Today, I'm going to talk, and you will listen."

Rishabh then grabbed Ishan with one hand and pulled him to a secluded corner. Even though the entire stadium was empty, Rishabh felt the need to find a private spot for their conversation.

"kya hai," Ishan said, irritated. "Stop acting dumb, Ishan. We both know that you aren't that stupid." "I do not know anything you are trying to say right now." "There is no way you didn't know that Shubman liked you. Everyone knew. I won't be surprised even if both your families knew about it." Rishabh ended his sentence with an intense look at Ishan.

"Rishabh I don't know what you are on about, and Shubman does not like me, okay? He might be confused right now because of his memories, but he doesn't like me, okay." "Are you trying to fool yourself right now? Are you serious?" Rishabh looked like he was losing too many of his brain cells during this conversation with Ishan at the moment.

"Of course, I am serious. Look, Shubman would laugh at his stupidity sometime later, and this would be a joke after he regains his memories." "Ishan, I don't know what is going on in your head right now, but don't demean Shubman's feelings like that. I don't care if you reciprocate his feelings or not, but please don't demean them. He likes you okay, and it isn't some kind of joke."

"Okay, shut up, Rishabh. He doesn't like me; I am demeaning nothing. He has lost his memories and is mistaking friendly affection for romantic feelings. That's all." "I have been quiet for so long just for Shubman, but not anymore. He has feelings for you, and we both know that he does, so stop playing already, okay? I don't know why you are pretending to not know about this, but please just stop already."

Rishabh was done with the conversation, and he couldn't lose any more brain cells behind this man anymore, so he decided to take the easy way out and leave the place for the time being. Ishan, on the other hand, had sat there an hour more before his head was clear enough for him to leave.

In their shared room, Shubman was peacefully sleeping when Ishan entered. He looked at Shubman's sleeping figure and quietly went to the washroom to freshen up. He came out of the bathroom after a good 30 minutes before climbing onto the bed beside Shubman. When under the covers, Ishan took a good look at Shubman's sleeping face, and something tugged at his heart, but he decided to ignore it and sleep the weirdness off.

Ishan was the first person to wake up in the morning, and he went to freshen up first. While he was still in the washroom, he could hear a movement from the other side of the room, indicating that Shubman had woken up too. Ishan felt like he didn't want to get out of the washroom because then it would mean confrontation, and he was in no shape for it right now. But he still decided to man the fuck up.

"Good morning." Shubman greeted Ishan as soon as he got out. When Ishan looked at him, he saw hesitation and fear in his eyes, and that broke something in Ishan. He was too angry at himself to even let Shubman feel like that. He wanted to hug and comfort Shubman, but he just couldn't bring himself to unintentionally hurt him anymore. So, he decided against it.

"Good morning, Shubi." Instead, he decided, to hell with awkwardness. There was anything but awkwardness between him and Shubman. "Are you still angry with me? You know what? Maybe you are right; maybe I am just thinking too much about it." Shubman tried to defend himself, but it broke Ishan even more.

"I was never angry, Shubi; I can never be angry at you. Just a bit overwhelmed, I think, but that's all. You need don't to be sorry about your feelings; I would never judge you." Ishan turned around to look at Shubman in hopes that he might find the sincerity of his words in his eyes. "I don't really know if I liked you before or not, but Ishan, when I say that I like you now, I mean it."

His words had so much weight in them that they scared Ishan. He really didn't know how to deal with this super bold shubman. So, he just smiled. "I think I am going to court you." And Ishan choked on plain air.

"What the fuck Shubman, where did that come from suddenly?" "Not suddenly. After you left yesterday, I thought a lot about it and realized that there is a fat chance that I might've always liked you. And since you know it already, I have lost my memories, and I desperately need them back. So, I decided to get to know you more, but not just as a friend or a best friend. If I am doing it, I should do it right."

"I decided that if we stop talking, I would never want you to hate or despise me because then my past self would hit me so hard that I would fall into the comma again, but if we still are talking, then I am sorry. I can't pretend that I don't find you the most interesting thing in the room. Or anywhere that is. So, I decided that I would court you. You have an option to reject me, of course, but that doesn't mean that I would stop."

Ishan couldn't process the drastic change between the timid and hesitant Shubman from 5 minutes ago and this bold and stupid Shubman at all. 'Court me, what the fuck do you think I am? A princess?' was the only thought Ishan could think of, and even before he could say anything, Shubman had already gone inside the bathroom, leaving a stunned Ishan alone to ponder his nonsense.

Title: The Stares
Chapter_Text:
"Ishan bhai, shubman bhai ko kya hua hai?" Rinku asked Ishan while giving Shubman a look, who was sitting just two seats away from them. In response to this strange question, Ishan turned his head to look at what exactly was wrong with Shubman, but just as he turned his head, he came eye to eye with a smiling Shubman. The first instinct of Ishan was to look away, but he endured it and maintained eye contact instead.

The more he looked at Shubman, the wider his smile got, and he was forced to look away at the end. Rinku was so confused that he turned to Rishabh for answers, who just smiled at him. "Are you all good?" Rishabh asked Ishan as he took a seat beside him. "Ha?" "kahan ghum hai?" There was a different kind of glint in Rishabh's eyes, and it scared Ishan a bit, "Kuch nahi."

"Shubman, Sun Teko Rohit Bhai bulare hai." Just then Siraj came from in front of them and asked for Shubman. "Shubman." He on the other hand, was too busy staring at 'someone'. "SHUBMAN." Siraj lost his last shred of patience, and his yell finally woke Shubman up from his la-la land. "kya hua bhai?" "Kitna ghurega tu usko? Bas kar, ab chal, Rohit bhai bula rahe hain." And they left, leaving behind a hooting crowd for Ishan to handle.

"Something Something?" asked Rishabh, winking at Ishan. He felt like smacking the stupidity out of him, but he put his attention on the current strategy instead. It had been too long since Shubman had disappeared with Siraj, and Ishan was now starting to worry. His problem? He couldn't openly show his concern, giving them another reason to hoot, so he kept his concerns to himself.

Later, Shubman returned to the field with other members. "Shubman will play the mock match tomorrow," Rohit informed the team, making an excited clatter go about. They were all more excited to welcome Shubman back on the team than Shubman himself was. Ishan couldn't help himself but give a bright smile to Rohit.

Shubman faked losing consciousness and fell sideways over Virat when he saw that smile. "Bachche pagal ho gaya hain?" Virat scolded him half-heartedly. "Wo Ishaan ki smile dekhke thoda behosh ho gaya, bhai, samjho na." Ishan glared at Rishabh and shook his head, grabbing his kit to leave the group as soon as possible. "Sachai se koi kab tak hi bhage ga." Shubman shouted behind his retreating figure, to which Ishaan replied with a middle finger.

The next day came by a bit too early for Ishan's liking. Now they had a mock match, and he wasn't ready for it at all. After getting ready he looked at Shubman who was busy staring at the strings of his shoes with too much passion. "What are you doing?" Startled by Ishan's sudden question, Shubman started staring at Ishan instead.

Ishan, on the other hand, just smiled at Shubman and bent down to tie his shoelace for him, stunning him. "No, I'll do it." "Don't be nervous. You'll do great. Trust me." Ishan said, completely ignoring Shubman's request to let go of the shoelace.

"Do you trust me?" Ishan asked once more when he got no response. "More than I trust myself." The intensity of that sentence made Ishan lose his footing for a bit. He just gave Shubman a small smile and left the room, leaving all his things behind. Shubman just smiled to himself before grabbing Ishan's and his kit to go downstairs to the team.

"Oho, Ishan, princess treatment, ha." "Shut up, Rishabh. You didn't have to bring it, Shubman. I could've brought it down myself." Ishan told Shubman while taking his kit from him. "Yeah, but you ran, leaving it behind. What else was I supposed to do?" "Leave it just how it was. What else?" Ishan gave the sass before walking behind Rohit. Rishabh and Shubman looked at each other and smiled fondly.

Everybody liked Ishan a bit too much for Shubman's liking. He was new to this feeling and couldn't help but wonder just how strong the bond of this team was. If it were to be the old Shubman, he would never feel bad about Ishan interacting with the members like this. That is because he knew all of them so well. To this Shubman it wasn't a pretty sight at all.

Later when the match started, Shubman had this sudden urge to prove himself. Earlier, he wanted to do well and make the best of this opportunity, but now he had this weird urge to do the best. To make Ishan proud. With the single thought of Ishan going through his mind, Shubman entered the field.

'let's do it,' was what he told himself before he went for a six on the very first ball. It was a bold decision, but Shubman was never the one to back out from a challenge. Soon the first six followed a second and a third, and suddenly Shubman was nearing his half-century. He was opening with Rohit, and Ishan was at one down. Right now, he was sitting in the dugout with the rest of the team.

Every time Shubman hit a six, he instinctively searched for Ishan in the crowd. It felt completely natural to him, and Shubman had no complaints. Soon, he reached the half-century mark, celebrating his first milestone after the accident. Once again, he scanned the stands for Ishan and felt a warm rush when he saw him looking like the happiest person in the world at that moment.

He was smiling too wide and was clapping enthusiastically. He gave him a smile when their eyes met, and Shubman felt like leaving everything he was doing behind and running towards him. 'Jaake bhi tu na jaye, thehri tu dil mein haye, hasrat si banke kyun bhala.' The wicketkeeper from behind the stumps started singing, suddenly forcing Shubman to jolt awake. Shubman gave him a look in response to which he just smiled like nothing happened.

Shubman had just smashed 79 runs off 33 balls, and he was too close to the century he deserved to back off now. His conscience urged him to play slowly and cautiously, but his hands weren't ready to cooperate. He glanced over at Rohit, hoping for support, but Rohit simply shook his head, a look of affection on his face, leaving Shubman to find his rhythm.

As he approached the century, Shubman's heart began to race. He felt a mix of anxiety, happiness, and nervousness all at once. With 98 runs on the board... he was out. Shubman aimed for a single, but he was stumped before he could reach the crease. It all happened too quickly for him to comprehend, and just like that, his first century after the accident remained elusive once again.

As he returned to the dugout, he was too upset to even look up. He felt that things could have been better if he had just opened his eyes. The disappointment was evident on Rohit's face, and Shubman was too scared to glance at Ishan. Just as he was about to enter the dugout, two strong arms wrapped around him in a firm but warm hug.

'The scent,' Shubman realized—it was Ishan. He hugged him back as tightly as he could, feeling as if one of their ribs might break if the embrace continued for much longer. But Shubman didn't want to let go; he longed to hold onto the moment forever. Eventually, he had to release the hug, meeting Ishan's gaze as he did so.

Ishan simply smiled at Shubman and mouthed, 'I am proud of you.' Shubman felt like his heart might burst from overwhelming emotions; he felt completely fulfilled as if he had just won the world. Suddenly, a distant yet vivid memory flashed through his mind—a cricket ground where he was surrounded by a group of people. He remembered a boy smiling at him, and it evoked a warm happiness that made him feel like he could die content.

"Shubi," Ishan's concerned voice pulled him back from his thoughts. After giving Shubman a firm squeeze on the shoulder, Ishan went to take his turn. Shubman settled into the dugout, realizing that watching Ishan play wasn't a bad option after all. He thought to himself, 'If for some reason I couldn't play in the future, watching Ishan would be therapy enough.'

Ishan, after completing his half-century, gave Shubman a smile from the ground, and Shubman felt like he could melt on the spot. "itna mat hans bhai, face muscles dukhenge." Rishabh came from behind him when he saw him sitting there smiling like an idiot. "Chup re." "Aha, ye acha hai, chup re to anyone who is not Ishan, right?"

Shubman didn't deny it; he loved listening to Ishan. Perhaps it was because he had always enjoyed it, or maybe it was because he wanted to learn more about him now that he had lost his memories. Regardless, he appreciated it nonetheless. Soon, Ishan came out after scoring a solid 62 runs and joined the team in the dugout.

And so, Shubman returned to his usual habit of staring. Ishan, exasperated, shot him a glare and said, "You do realize there are cameras here, right?" This sudden remark made Shubman look around, while Rishabh tsked in response, "As if he's ever cared about the cameras before."

Virat, sitting beside them, let out a hearty laugh. "Well, Ishi baby, he has always been staring at you; the whole world knows, hahaha!"

Ishan turned to Virat in stunned silence before glancing back at the crowd. It was true that Shubman was often the one initiating these stares, but at that moment, he hadn't confessed his feelings yet. This made it seem like they were just friendly glances, but now it felt different, and it scared Ishan.

Title: Lunch?
Chapter_Text:
"Can you stop staring at me?" Ishan asked again, still avoiding eye contact with Shubman. "No," came Shubman's response, loud and clear, with no hint of hesitation. The answer stunned Ishan, making him turn to look at Shubman. "What?" Ishan replied, puzzled. "What? You asked me if I could stop staring, and I just answered you," Shubman said calmly. "What's wrong with you?" Ishan questioned.

"Nothing at all. I'm doing very well, actually," Shubman replied with a smirk. "Stop looking at me," Ishan insisted. "Why? Does it make you feel things?" Shubman teased. "What?" Ishan exclaimed, taken aback by the unexpected remark. He was shocked and momentarily speechless.

Shubman shook his head and laughed at Ishan's confused state, clearly enjoying the moment. "It definitely makes him feel things," Rishabh chimed in, letting out a throaty laugh as he gave Shubman a high five.

"This is bullying," Ishan said, feeling utterly defeated. "But it's just love," Virat added from behind him, and Ishan realized that nobody was on his side at that moment. Later, after the mock match ended and they had won, the team returned to their hotel to relax and prepare for the actual tournament.

Once back, Ishan went straight to his room. After sitting on the bed, he decided to watch something and maybe scroll through some reels before freshening up. So, he opened the app he deemed cursed—Instagram. He had named it that because there always seemed to be something unpleasant popping up on it.

As he scrolled, he came across a reel of him and Shubman in the dugout from earlier that day, and he cursed under his breath. 'Not this again. How fast do these people work?' he wondered, continuing to scroll.

Then he saw a reel of himself hugging Shubman after he had nearly scored a century. Now that he thought about it, he regretted hugging him so suddenly; he had just been unable to bear Shubman's sad expression without trying to do something to cheer him up.

Shubman had always been this way; he appeared to be a laid-back guy, but up close, he could be frustrating. He had a tendency to blame every failure on himself and linger in his guilt indefinitely, which Ishan found exasperating. He hated it when Shubman would retreat into a cocoon of self-loathing and refuse to accept help from anyone.

When asked how he was doing, Shubman always replied with, "I am okay." Deep down, Ishan wished he would admit that he wasn't okay and that everything wasn't fine. Ishan had always been the type to overlook when someone wronged him, preferring to let things go. In contrast, Shubman continuously urged him to stand up for himself and retaliate against insults.

When it came to being loose-mouthed and foolish, Shubman was number one. However, when it came to his feelings, he often acted like an idiot. Rather than accepting that he wasn't okay, he would gaslight himself into believing he was fine, and Ishan always noticed. He could tell when Shubman was plunging into those episodes of self-loathing; it had become second nature for him to see it.

During those times, his instinct was to sit beside Shubman. He wouldn't say a word, just be there to let him know that he was present—that he would always be there for him. Ishan wanted Shubman to understand that no matter how badly he might mess up, he wasn't going anywhere.

Looking back, Ishaan realized he had always sensed that Shubman felt more than just friendship; perhaps he had been in denial about it. Regardless, that was all in the past now, and he preferred not to dwell on it.

Nevertheless, he often found himself thinking about those feelings. There was no way he could have missed them; everyone else seemed to know as well. Ishan found himself reflecting on how Shubman might have felt all along, grappling with so many hidden emotions.

Did he feel sadness? Was he overthinking? Ishan was certain that he didn't love Shubman in any way other than as a friend, yet when he looked at him, it stirred something deep in his heart. He had always been aware that Shubman liked to gaze at him; it was almost a habit, but he had never inquired about the reason behind it.

Now that he understood, he couldn't stop his mind from wandering or his heart from racing under the intensity of those stares. He couldn't help but wonder when and how Shubman's feelings for him had begun, and most importantly, why him. Out of all the people, why did Shubman choose him?

Shubman could easily attract anyone, and that was no exaggeration. Ishan had always been vocal about how handsome he thought Shubman was— because he truly was. Handsome, charming, and a millionaire cricketer, what more could someone want? He could have had anyone, so why Ishan? These thoughts had consumed Ishan ever since Shubman's confession. Did Shubman always like men?

'But I would've known if he had. He would've told me, right? Was he scared that I would judge him?' That thought frightened Ishan. He didn't want to imagine a scenario where Shubman felt he might be judged by him. He would never do that—at least not in this lifetime. Ishan thought he had made it clear to Shubman how he felt, but alas, one can never fully understand another's feelings.

As he looked at the video again, he noticed how Shubman physically relaxed during that hug. He could see the relief on Shubman's face when he told him he was proud of him. Ishan wanted to see that smile forever; he wanted to hold on to that memory and never let it go.

Lost in these thoughts, Ishan involuntarily smiled. "What got you smiling like that?" he heard. Startled awake from his daydream, Ishan jumped a little. Later, when he analysed the moment, he realized that Shubman had just walked into the room and caught him off guard.

Trying to cover up, he said, "Nothing. Just talking to Aditi." And he slapped himself internally. "Who?" Shubman looked and sounded very upset at the mention of her, and Ishan felt like teasing him a bit more. "Aditi? You don't know her? Oh right, your memories... She is a 'close' friend that I met at a party a few months back. I enjoy her company quite a lot."

"Righttt, Aditi. I don't care who she is at all, Ishan, but remember that I am the one courting you right now." Ishan, after seeing that sad look on Shubman's face after the mention of Aditi, didn't expect this answer at all.

"what?" "What?" Shubman repeated. "I never agreed on your courting or whatever." "I make the rules, baby. Come on, get ready; we are going out for lunch." It wasn't the first time Shubman was using that endearment, but it was mostly used by Ishan before.

After the confession, though, that word had a completely different meaning now, and Ishan was too embarrassed at it. "we are not going anywhere." "Yes, we are. We are going on lunch, so please get ready."

After a lot of arguments, Ishan finally gave in and begrudgingly got up to go get ready. Shubman smiled in victory. When both of them were ready to leave, they went downstairs. And the most irritating part of Ishan's day started. The hootings. "Ohoooo, is Ishi going somewhere?" Hardik was the first to notice them.

Ishan just glared at him and then started glaring at Shubman. "What did I do?" He defended himself, acting too innocent for Ishan's liking. "Bhai, Shubman se nazar hate toh kahi aur bhi dekh lena." Rishabh joined the drama. "Tum sab jao bhaad main main kahi nahi jara ab."

With that, Ishan turned around to go back to his room but was pulled into a hug by Shubman. "A commitment is a commitment, baby; you cannot back off now," he whispered in his ear, loud enough for the crowd to hoot in return. And Ishan was left embarrassed yet again. Shubman took Ishan's hand and dragged him out of the hooting crowd and into the car.

The ride to the destination was silent, and nobody spoke anything, letting the calmness elope them in a thin bubble of serenity. Neither of them wanted to break this silenced calmness. They sat there enjoying the night and each other's company. After a while, Shubman stopped the car and motioned for Ishan to get out.

Shubman went to park the car and came back with the biggest smile on his face. "what?" Ishan asked, "Nothing, just happy to be here with you." And Ishan would die with the fact that he melted a bit at that. They went inside the restaurant and took a seat at the far corner.

"Did you book it in advance?" Ishan asked Shubman. "yes." Shubman replied with sparkling eyes. There weren't many people in the restaurant; perhaps it was more famous for dinner, Ishan thought. After a minute or two the waitress came in to take the order. "he will order." Shubman pointed towards Ishan.

Ishan left confused, took the menu under pressure, and ordered a few things. "Why the sudden lunch plan?" "Because I am courting you?" "What nonsense, Shubman." "Okay, I want to know more about you. Tell me, do you like tea or coffee?"

Ishan, after witnessing that this man was not in the mood to give up at all, decided to play along. "It took you years to know me as well as you did. Now you need another couple of years to know me better. You cannot just ask me about it; Shubi, do some hard work and research."

Shubman just smiled at it. "Now you are talking." And Ishan just laughed at it. He realized that after all, this was his best friend, Shubman. There was nothing in the world that could keep him from talking to him or indulging in his stupidity.

Title: She likes you.
Chapter_Text:
"Do you like shrimps?" Shubman asked when he saw Ishan enjoying the shrimps that he had ordered, a bit too much. "I love seafood." The statement was as plain as it could be, but it still tugged something at Shubman's heart.

Earlier, when he had looked at the menu and had asked Ishan to order, he had been prepared for a lot of seafood on the table for some reason. He felt happy to know that his instincts still remembered Ishan so well. He knew that Ishan liked seafood well enough for him to order it.

"Earth to Shubman." He came back to reality when he heard the voice that was so close to his heart. He smiled at Ishan, and his heart skipped a beat when he smiled back at him. His heart was skipping a bit too many beats these days. It might be very unhealthy, but is Shubman considering being healthy right now? No. No, he wasn't. Ishan has taken all the space in his mind, and Shubman doesn't think about anything that isn't Ishan these days.

The lunch was as simple as it could get. There was nothing too fancy about it, but for Shubman it was better than anything else he had experienced. He loved every minute of this setting. Ishan and he had talked a lot more than they usually did. Ishan seemed relaxed; nothing about him suggested that he was uncomfortable.

Shubman at first was unwilling to ask him out on a date. He felt like it could be the reason Ishan would finally snap at him, but being here like this, Shubman felt like even dying would have been worth the try. He wanted to be here in this moment forever. Listen to Ishan Yap forever.

It was so weird because Shubman was so lost in Ishan's eyes that he could barely hear anything from around him, but somehow he heard and understood every word that Ishan was speaking. It was scary, but did Shubman care? No, he didn't.

"What is it?" Ishan asked out of nowhere. "What?" stunned, Shubman asked. "I know you want to ask something, so just do it. Tell me what is bothering you." Now that Shubman thought about it, that was very true. He did have something to ask indeed. How Ishan knew about it was a topic that Shubman would discuss with himself later, but for now, he needed to organize his terrain of thoughts and sort out an answer to Ishan's question.

"Well, the thing is... I thought that maybe this lunch would be awkward, you know. You didn't want to come, but I forced you anyway. I was prepared for the silent treatment and quiet awkwardness. But this, this is anything but that. It feels a bit too natural."

That was true. Shubman had made up his mind for the complete opposite of what was going on here. He didn't expect the conversations to be as smooth as they were now, and he absolutely didn't expect Ishan to be this easygoing here. He thought that Ishan would be a bit stubborn and unsettling.

"Well, it feels natural because it is. You might not remember it, but we used to come out like this all the time. 'Friendly dates' you called it." Ishan smiled at the memory, and Shubman's heart did another somersault. "Yeah, I am sure they were friendly." This comment of Shubman's made Ishan look up at him. There was something in his eyes that Shubman couldn't just understand.

"Well, I don't know about that, but they were the best part of my days. Shubman, I don't care about whatever. I don't care if they were just friendly for you or not; nothing would change the fact that I have spent the majority of my life in your company, and trust me when I say it: I enjoyed every minute of it."

All the overthinking that Ishan had done earlier at the hotel was playing with his mind. He couldn't just let things be and make Shubman believe that he didn't care about him. He couldn't fathom the fact that Shubman thought this lunch would be awkward. Ishan had never felt awkward with Shubman, and he never will. And he was on a mission to make Shubman understand that.

"Shubman, I love spending time with you. I love every second of our conversations. Look, I don't care how you think your feelings are for me. But to me, you will always be special. It doesn't have to be in a romantic way, you know." "I know." Was the only thing Shubman could say before the calm silence enveloped them again.

When they reached the hotel a few hours later, the team was still loitering around in the lobby. They received the eyes when they entered, and Ishan was done with all of them at this point. "what?" he asked. "You seem to enjoy using that word a bit too much these days," Rishabh replied, looking up from his laptop. "Oh. Shubi, Sara came to see you." He continued before Ishan could get back at him.

Hearing that Sara came to see him, Shubman was confused for a second before asking, "How was my relationship with her?" "You were close. She liked you." Ishan said, looking straight at Shubman, that stare made Shubman increasingly aware of Ishan's intentions to set him up with her, and it made him sad beyond measure.

"so?" Shubman deadpanned. "Nothing. I was just saying." Ishan shrugged under Shubman's scrutinizing gaze. Later when Ishan looked up, he realized that everyone was looking at him with disappointment in their eyes. "What is wrong with you? She liked him, and I just told him about it because he asked." He left the room with that.

"Does he want me to end up with her, or does he just want me to not like him anymore?" "Neither. He is just an idiot." Yashasvi replied without even blinking, and everybody agreed with him. "You should talk to Sara, though." And Shubman agreed to talk to her. He would meet her tomorrow.

Ishan in the room weighed a lot of emotions in his heart. So much that he couldn't pinpoint any of them. He just lay down on the bed and drifted off to sleep without even realizing it.

Ishan woke up the next day with an empty bed. There was no sign of Shubman in the room, the bathroom, or the balcony. Ishan shouldn't have been as stressed about his sudden disappearance as he was. But that was a concern for the future Ishan. So, he did the right thing. He freshened up and went downstairs to ask about him to the team.

"Where is Shubman?" "Did he not tell you before leaving?" "I didn't meet him last night at all. I slept before he came to the room and just woke up." "Right, he didn't have the chance to tell you." "Where is he?" They exchanged a look that Ishan didn't know the meaning of before replying to him in a solemn voice, "He went to meet Sara."

"Suddenly?" "What's suddenly? She came to meet him twice already and couldn't find him, so yeah, it is only right that he meet her now, right?" "Right." And Ishan left with that. His expressions were unreadable. "Do you see something amiss?" Rishabh asked Siraj with twinkling eyes.

"Not as much as I expected to see, but this is good too." "What do you mean, Miya? This is already too much. You expected too much from a dumb person like Ishan." "Well, that is also true." And they shared a hearty laugh at that, completely unaware of Ishan and his turmoil.

"I came to see you yesterday, but you were out," Sara told Shubman. They were both sitting in a restaurant nearby. "Yes, I was out with Ishan." And with that, her expression changed. "Look, you might know already that I have no memories whatsoever of the past ten years or so. I am so sorry, but I don't remember anything about you either." "I know. I mean, if you couldn't remember Ishan, how can anybody expect that you remember them?"

"What?" Shubman was stunned at that sentence. Sara just laughed out loud. "You seem to be confused. Do you really remember nothing? Well, Ishan is your best friend that you know of, right?" "Yeah, they told me." "So, you were with him all this time? How did you like him?" "He is an amazing person. I can completely see why he was my best friend." "Just that?" Sara asked, confusing Shubman more. "what else?"

"Nothing. Tell me, Shubman, what did they tell you about me?" Shubman chuckled before replying. "they said that you were my PR stunt." And Sara laughed at that too. "that is true." "and Ishan said that I should go on a date with you. I feel like he is trying his best to set me up with you for some reason."

"He is an idiot." She replied, making Shubman laugh once more. "all the teammates said the same thing." "Because he is." "He said that you like me." Shubman was a bit hesitant in saying that because he didn't know how Sara would react, but he didn't want to lead her on either; he just wanted to clear things up.

"Are we doing this again?" "Again?" questioned Shubman. "Well, I seem to remember a similar conversation between us. Let me guess: you want to tell me that I might be an amazing person and might as well be the most beautiful girl, but you cannot give me a chance; you, in fact, don't want to. All because you are in love with someone else and you cannot love anyone who is not him? So, you would rather not lead me on?"

Sara said all that with so much confidence that even Shubman thought that the conversation might as well be scripted. He was actually trying to say exactly that. "Ho..w?" Sara smiled at the stunned Shubman.

"You have already told me once, and yeah, I did like you back then, but not anymore. I am rooting for you and your undying love for Ishan." Shubman was stunned even more. "how'd you know?" "Do you think I am blind? You never really told me who this person is that you love, but trust me, it wasn't hard to guess at all."

And Shubman just looked down, embarrassed. He hadn't been this embarrassed a day since he had woken up in that hospital bed. "I am surprised that the person who calls himself your best friend couldn't guess it. or wait, maybe he isn't just dense and blind but is in denial." "I told him." "WHAT?" She shouted so loud that the tables near them took a look at them.

"What?" she asked again in a lowered voice. "Did you confess to him?" "I didn't mean to; I just wanted to know if I always liked him, or was I falling after losing my memory, and it just slipped like that?" "What are you doing about it then?" "I am...um...courting him." And Sara looked at him speechless. After a good 5 minutes of stunned silence, she started to laugh so hard that Shubman was concerned about her mental health for a second there.

"You are doing what? What is this, Shubman? 500 bc?" "Stop laughing like that, okay? Is it that wrong to try and court someone?" "Oh god, Shubman, stop using that word. Did Ishan not slap you when you told him that?" "He very much wanted to." And they both shared a laugh at that.

Title: I Miss The Old You.
Chapter_Text:
Ishan had been sitting on the balcony for the past thirty minutes, unsure of why he was there. Was it because of Sara? He had always liked the idea of Sara and Shubman together. He even tried to play wingman for them once, but it ended unsuccessfully. Shubman was adamant about not considering it. He had consistently avoided the topic as if it were a plague.

Ishan found Shubman's behavior strange. The Shubman Gill he knew wouldn't let a pretty girl slip away so easily. Shubman was known for being a notorious flirt. Initially, Ishan didn't appreciate this aspect of him, but he later realized that Shubman was just a flirt and didn't mean any harm. However, being as charismatic as Shubman was, he inevitably broke quite a few hearts.

In recent years, though, Shubman had distanced himself from any form of relationship. He stopped talking to girls, blocked all of his contacts, and visibly recoiled at the sight of anyone flirting with him. This wasn't the Shubman Ishan remembered, and it left him feeling quite unsettled.

This situation began after Sara entered the scene, leading Ishan to believe that Shubman might have feelings for her. However, Shubman denied this every time the topic was raised. While Ishan might not be the most perceptive in these matters, he was certain of one thing: Shubman Gill was in love. The only question was with whom.

For an entire year, Shubman dismissed any claims about being in love, especially after a noticeable change in his behavior. There were no girls, no flirting, and essentially no romantic interests at all. He insisted that he was on a journey of self-purification. Initially, Ishan thought Shubman was going through another phase, but as months went by, Shubman remained unchanged. This began to raise eyebrows among their friends, but Shubman's responses never wavered.

After a full year, Ishan decided to sit Shubman down and ask him what was wrong. Shubman smiled but chose not to respond. Eventually, everyone started to speculate that Shubman might actually be in love, and he stopped denying these claims. Instead, he would just smile whenever someone teased him about it.

It had been too long since Ishan last saw Shubman with anyone at all. Now that he added two and two, it was very possible that Shubman liked him. But Ishan was still in denial. There was this image at the back of Ishan's mind. An image where Shubman, after regaining his memories, would deny being in love with him. He would say that he liked someone indeed, but it wasn't him.

Ishan was so sure that this Shubman was mistaken that he couldn't interpret those eyes of love that Shubman looked at him with. If only Ishan could open his eyes, he would see that Shubman didn't change at all. He behaved the same as he did before; he just gave a label to what was happening. Shubman's feelings didn't change at all; they just had a name now.

But he wasn't thinking about that at all. He wasn't even thinking about Shubman. What occupied his mind was Sara. Earlier, when he learned that Shubman had gone to meet her, he felt a strange tug at his heart, and he couldn't quite understand it. Ishan had seen Shubman meet her countless times before, and all he had felt was happiness and playful teasing. This time, however, it was different.

Ishan wanted to act nonchalantly, just like he had in the past, but he couldn't ignore his feelings any longer. What could they possibly be talking about? Would Shubman change his mind when he returned? Wouldn't it be better if he did? Ishan and Shubman could go back to being best friends, and Shubman could give Sara a chance. Wasn't that what Ishan wanted? Wasn't that exactly what he desired?

Was he scared of something? What could he possibly be afraid of?

"ISHAN." A sudden buzz in his ear jolted him, making it feel as though he might go deaf. His head pounded as he snapped back to reality, seeing Rishabh standing over him with a phone in hand. "What is wrong with you?" Ishan asked, annoyance evident in his voice.

"What's wrong with me? What's wrong with you? Are you okay? I've been calling for you for the past 15 minutes. Your phone rang at least five times, and you didn't notice. Ishan, are you alright?" "Why were you calling me? You could have just come here and told me."

"Have you completely lost it, Ishan? Your mom has been trying to reach you. When you didn't pick up, she called Shubman. He was out, so he called me, and now I find you sitting here, lost in thought. What on earth were you even thinking about?" "Oh." "Yeah, oh. Now go and call your mom before she freaks out."

Rishabh left the room after that."Yeah... fucking hell, Shubman Gill, he is alive. Can you stop calling me for like a second? This is your 72nd call in the past 15 minutes. Is that even possible to call as many times in 15 fucking minutes?" Ishan could hear Rishabh shouting over the call. It was probably Shubman he was shouting at. And the thought made Ishan smile before he shook his head and took his phone to make a call to his mom.

Shubman came back from meeting Sara in the afternoon and joined the team at lunch. "how did it go?" Siraj asked when he saw Shubman coming towards them. "better than expected. She is sweet." "how'd you know? Did you taste her?" Rishabh deadpanned. "Do you want to taste blood?" Shubman got back at him. "you..." he then pointed at Ishan, who was calmly eating his meal.

"What did I do?" asked a stunned Ishan. "Why would you not pick up your calls? You think it is funny to be so nonchalant?" "I... I am sorry I didn't see it. My phone was on the bedside table, and I was on the balcony." "Yeah, right, and he couldn't even hear people shouting in his ears," Rishabh said through a mouthful of food, making Rohit glare at him.

Shubman, after glaring at Ishan for a good 5 minutes, took a seat beside him. "So, you wanna meet her again," Ishan asked Shubman after he had sat down. Shubman gave him an unimpressed look. "If you want me to stop liking you and start going on dates with girls to 'explore,' it is not happening, okay? I like you, and you cannot change that fact even if you try to." Shubman said while stealing food from him.

Ishan just looked at Shubman with those unreadable eyes again. Sometimes Shubman couldn't read Ishan at all. These were the times he felt utterly helpless. He wanted to know the meaning of Ishan's every gaze and every smile, but sometimes he just couldn't do it, so he just looked down, breaking eye contact.

What Shubman didn't know was that sometimes it was hard for Ishan to sort his feelings too. Even he couldn't figure out what was going on sometimes, and this was one of those times. They couldn't understand the simplest of things sometimes, and it was getting under Ishan's skin at this point.

The conversation was then smartly brought upon that one fateful day when Shubman decided to confess. And everyone shared a laugh at the memory while Shubman was left embarrassed, but he still owned up to it. "Look, I didn't mean to confess like that. It was an accident." "Yeah right, saying, 'Was I romantically into you since before I lost my memories, or have I started losing my mind behind you now?' was accidental indeed."

Rishabh had mastered the technique to get on Shubman's nerves. He wanted to slap the man, but he held himself back. "Why do you even remember his exact words?" Ishan said after a while. "Are you defending him now?" Siraj asked, surprised. "What is that question, Miya? Of course, Ishan would defend him." Virat had joined in the teasing too.

"Virat bhai? I thought you were on my team." Shubman faked the tears, and Ishan smiled that big smile of his that was reserved for Shubman somehow. "but really, I like this Shubman better than the old one. This one is so fucking bold it is damn entertaining." Rishabh said making everyone agree with him.

"True, that is, but old Shubman was amazing too, no? Not bold in this particular thing, but yeah, his fears were genuine." Virat backed Shubman up. "I miss my old Shubi." And the moment this sentence left Ishan's mouth, the entire room snapped their neck towards him. It was a miracle how none of them cracked their necks.

"What is wrong with you'll? I have spent the majority of my time with Shubi; of course, I miss him now. Let me remind you that he is my best friend." "But you have Shubi still, don't you?" Shubman asked that with so much innocence that Ishan's heart started to melt. "Well, yes, I do, but old Shubi was different; he was my Shubi."

"I have a question, guys... Is it possible for a person to be jealous of his own self?" This sentence of Shubman's made everyone laugh until death. There was laughter all around the room. Some were clutching their stomachs while they threw their heads back and laughed out loud; some were smiling fondly at Shubman.

And Ishan just looked at Shubman with those unreadable eyes again. Only this time one emotion could clearly be seen amongst all the other ones. It was adoration. Ishan adored Shubman, and it was a known fact. Only this time it was too visible in his eyes to ignore anymore.

Title: He Fainted?
Chapter_Text:
Ishan was sitting on the balcony of his room, reflecting on his recent return to Patna to stay with his parents after the tournament. The tournament had been a great success for India and especially for Ishan, who had scored two consecutive centuries and felt very proud of himself. Although he wouldn't say it out loud, the person he credited most for his outstanding performance was Shubman and his better health.

Ishan had not performed well in the last two tournaments, both of which coincided with Shubman being in the hospital. During the third tournament, in a moment of impulsive decision-making, he left everything behind and returned to India. Looking back, he realized that it was a rather crazy decision, and he considered it a miracle that the board didn't penalize him for it.

He had been so troubled by everything that he had stopped thinking clearly— in fact, he had stopped thinking altogether. However, he began to realize that the people around him understood him better than he had assumed. Ishan was extremely grateful to all of them. Ishan had been severely affected by shubman's accident and it was a known fact even if he wanted to deny it.

The tournament had ended a week ago, and he had arrived in Patna the very next day. The team had insisted that he join them in Mumbai, but he wanted some time alone, away from everything that stressed him out. As a result, he had politely declined their offers.

Shubman was not allowed to play in the international match yet, but his presence during the practice sessions and the main tournament was crucial for his career. Ishan was glad that Shubman could still join the team in the dugouts, even though he wasn't part of the squad.

Ishan had also turned down the offer to travel with the team because he didn't want to overthink various issues. Ironically, he found himself doing just that while sitting at home. There was no difference in his situation. Most of the thoughts that troubled him somehow revolved around Shubman. At this point, Ishan felt frustrated with himself.

Ishan found himself frequently thinking about Shubman, even when they were apart, and it was becoming increasingly difficult to ignore. This wasn't the first time he had experienced this; however, the intensity of these thoughts was unsettling. The more Ishan contemplated the situation, the more he wished the earth would swallow him whole.

He had never imagined that he would one day sit and question his own sanity over thoughts of Shubman. Ishan had always kept Shubman in his mind, yet he never considered that best friends might not constantly ponder each other's lives. Perhaps best friends didn't need to know every detail about one another.

Ishan had always believed their interactions were typical of best friends—until Shubman shattered his comfortable delusion by confessing his feelings. That confession rattled Ishan to his core, compelling him to re-evaluate his entire perspective on their relationship. Suddenly, all the small, tender moments they had shared took on a new significance in Ishan's mind.

He wanted to convince himself that it might not be the case at all, that maybe Shubman was just acting out because of his temporary dementia. He hoped that all the moments they shared were as natural as they felt, that their experiences together as friends were truly nothing more than that.

But who was he kidding? Ishan could be as clueless and oblivious as a koala, yet even he couldn't ignore the reality that their bond was far deeper than mere platonic friendship. Ishan had realized this long before today, but he chose to deceive himself into believing otherwise.

Ishan was lost in thought when he heard a soft knock on his door. "Come in," he said absent-mindedly. Pallavi entered the room, holding his phone. "Ishu, you left your phone downstairs after lunch. Shubman's mom called, but the call was declined before I could answer. You should talk to her; it might be something urgent." She handed him the phone, flashed him a smile, and left the room before he could thank her.

Ishan opened his call log, wondering why Shubman's mom had called him. "Hello?" he said as she picked up. For a few seconds, there was no response, just dead silence. Ishan started to worry that there might be a network issue. Just as he contemplated ending the call, he heard an unmistakable sob from the other end.

"Hello, Aunty? What's wrong?" Ishan said, now feeling anxious. He could hear some commotion in the background, and soon he recognized Shahneel's voice. "Hello, Ishan. Don't worry too much; it's just Shubman who fainted, and Mom started calling everyone in a panic."

"SHUBMAN WHAT?" The blood rushed to Ishan's head, and he couldn't think straight for a moment. "Relax, Ishii. It's nothing; he was probably just remembering some forgotten memories. It's okay; we took him to the hospital. He'll be alright," Shahneel tried to calm Ishan down, but he could hear the worry in her tone.

Ishan could see through the situation. There was no way Shubman's mom would call him out of the blue just because she panicked. Shahneel would never sound so concerned over something trivial. It had to be a much bigger issue, and Shahneel was just trying to keep him from worrying. "Okay, I'm on my way," he said, canceling the call before Shahneel could say anything else.

Ishan quickly searched for the earliest plane tickets and grabbed a random bag, filling it with his essentials. About an hour later, he came downstairs, bag in hand. "Where are you going?" Pallavi asked, eyeing the bag. "Shubman has fainted."

The moment he spoke, his family abandoned everything they were doing to ask him what he meant. They all looked just as worried as Ishan. "I don't know much, and Shahneel Di has said that it isn't something to worry about, but I can feel the tension. I have a bad feeling about this."

"Do you want me to book the tickets for you?" Raj asked, picking up his phone from the side table. "It's okay; I already booked the tickets." "Are they in Mumbai?" his mom asked, concern etched on her face. "Yes. I remember Shubman mentioning that they had all moved there for the time being. I'm sure that's where they are right now."

"And which hospital is he admitted to?" his father asked. "It has to be the same one as before since he gets his routine check-ups there after the accident." "When is your flight scheduled?" Pallavi inquired. "In about two hours," he replied. "You should get going then," she said after hearing his answer. "I'll drive you to the airport," Raj offered as he grabbed his keys.

"Ishan," his mom called out just as he was about to get into the car. "Yeah?" he responded. "Don't worry too much. Maybe they're right; perhaps it isn't anything to be overly concerned about. Call us after you arrive. We'll come by later," she reassured him. "Okay." With that, he left home.

While he was still on the flight, Ishan received a phone call from Virat. "Hello, bhai?" "Are you coming to Mumbai?" "I'm on my way, yes. Why?" "Well, you could have come tomorrow; we were here to take care of things for the time being. Don't stress too much, okay? Just get here safely."

Ishan recognized the same tone in Virat's voice as he had heard from his mother earlier. He was not a child to be so careless; he knew how to get there safely. What Ishan hadn't realized was the extent of his worry for Shubman and its implications.

Ishan didn't understand it himself, but everyone had witnessed his reaction when Shubman first had the accident months earlier. The doctors weren't even sure they could save him. Since then, Ishan had made it a routine to stay at the hospital for as long as he could each day.

He had neglected his practice and completely given up on everything unrelated to Shubman. This indifference severely affected his performance, but who could say anything to him about it? Even the public took notice. For the first time in Indian cricket history, a player was not trolled for poor performance, even during such an important series. Ishan looked so exhausted that even his detractors felt sympathy for him at that moment.

Now that he had fainted again, the team was reluctant to go through it all once more. They knew that Ishan was blaming himself for the incident. They had made it clear that it wasn't his fault, but he was stubbornly reluctant to accept that. Ishan hadn't explicitly stated that he felt responsible for the accident, but those around him could see it nonetheless.

Title: Ishan?
Chapter_Text:
As soon as Ishan arrived at the hospital, the nurse guided him to the room where Shubman was being kept under observation. It took Ishan three hours to fly and another hour by cab to reach the hospital, but Shubman still hadn't awakened. "What's wrong?" Ishan asked as he joined the other members of the group.

"Nothing. Everything is fine," Hardik replied too quickly. Ishan looked at Hardik with a questioning gaze that seemed to say, 'I want the truth, not this "everything is fine" nonsense.' "Really, Ishan, you can speak to the doctors if you want," Virat interjected. "Yeah, thank you," Ishan said, and he went to find the doctor in charge of Shubman's care.

The doctor explained, "There's nothing we can say for sure at this moment, but he is not in danger. He collapsed due to a sudden rush of memories, and he will wake up when his brain is ready to process them again. It's similar to how your phone sometimes shuts down because of software issues. I specifically instructed everyone not to trigger his memories deliberately and to allow him to regain them at his own pace."

"I'm relieved that he has regained them naturally. Had those memories been forced, he might have ended up in a persistent vegetative state. Since they have come back to him at his own pace, there's no need to worry about his condition. However, I can't estimate how long it will take for him to wake up. I assure you it won't be more than a few weeks."

"A FEW WEEKS?" Ishan exclaimed, his mouth agape and his eyes wide as saucers. "Well, that is the estimated timeframe; it doesn't mean he will be unconscious for that long. He might wake up in a few days—or even a few hours—but nothing can be said for sure."

"How is that different from being in a coma?" "His brain is responding like a normal unconscious person's would, so he isn't in any immediate danger."

When Ishan approached the doctor to inquire about Shubman's condition, he hadn't considered this possibility at all. He had come prepared for the worst-case scenario, and what the doctor had shared was far from the terrible outcome he imagined. Yet, strangely enough, it didn't ease his anxiety. Ishan wished the doctor would say that Shubman might wake up in an hour or that he was already awake, just under observation.

He couldn't bring himself to believe in this vegetative state nonsense. The rational part of his mind kept reminding him that, at least, Shubman wasn't in danger and that he would be okay. But, in that moment, Ishan found it hard to think rationally. He didn't want to be rational; he simply wanted to see Shubman wake up and be alright.

"Don't listen to him; it's his hobby to scare people," a gentle voice said from behind him. When Ishan turned around, he was faced with a calm expression belonging to a female doctor.

"What?" he asked. "Don't worry about what he said. When they first brought the patient in, he told the family that Shubman's brain wasn't responding and that they might have caused this condition. Poor mother nearly fainted."

She glanced at the retreating figure of the doctor she had just spoken about, disappointment evident in her eyes. "He's not a good person, really. I feel bad for his patients. But trust me, he's an excellent doctor. Your friend will wake up in no more than three days under his care. It could even happen in a few hours. So, don't worry too much."

"He told Shubman's mother that they might have caused this state for her son," Ishan added. The doctor chuckled lightly. "Yes, he did. Believe me, I wanted to slap him too." "Where are they now?" Ishan asked, almost in a daze. He recalled the phone call from Shubman's mom, her voice shaking.

"They're in the family room. I suggested they rest for a bit. The boys arrived a few hours ago, but the family has been here much longer. I wanted them to go home for a while, but they insisted on staying. You should try to convince them to go home; I think they'd listen to you." She smiled warmly and pointed in the direction of the family room.

Ishan walked towards the room, not fully aware of his surroundings. He could hear the words being spoken, but he couldn't process them; he simply followed the suggested direction in a daze. "Are you okay?" He turned to see the same doctor holding his upper arm, looking at him with concern. "Yeah." That was all he could muster at the moment.

When he had arrived at the hospital earlier, he could still form complete sentences and think clearly, but now it felt like that ability had slipped away from him. After observing Ishan for a minute or two, the doctor called over a nurse and handed her the file she was holding. Without a word, she guided Ishan toward a door marked 'Family Room.'

As he entered, the first thing that caught his eye was Shahneel, wiping away her tears. Next, he noticed Shubman's mother asleep on a couch, while his father stood over her, texting someone. The doctor cleared her throat behind him, and as soon as she had their attention, she quietly exited the room, closing the door behind her.

Shahneel ran up and hugged him tightly, while Shubman's father gently led them both out of the room to avoid disturbing the sleeping mother. "You came," Shahneel exclaimed; it wasn't a question, but rather a given. Of course, he came. Ishan had never seen Shahneel this upset before; even when Shubman had gotten into the accident, Shahneel had remained the calmest person in the room.

"What's wrong?" Ishan asked, concerned. "Nothing. It was just all a bit too overwhelming." He sat there with them, asking about how they had found him unconscious. After a while, Shahneel's mother came out of the room, panicking when she found no one there with her upon waking up.

It took some time to console her; she looked frantic. Once she calmed down and saw Ishan standing there, she hugged him immediately. Ishan comforted her and convinced both of them to leave the hospital for now. He assured Shubman's parents that he would take care of everything and that they should go get some rest.

The doctor had been right after all; he successfully managed to send them home when everyone else had tried and failed. They listened to him without question. Shahneel had to be somewhere important but had been late because of this accident, so she left to wrap up her work before returning to the hospital.

It had been three hours since Ishan arrived at the hospital, and time felt both too fast and too slow. He couldn't comprehend a word anyone was saying, and the entire episode of Shubman's family leaving went by in a daze. He didn't remember what any of them had said; in fact, he didn't even recall what he himself had said.

He was tired and hungry, but he also felt nauseous and sleepless. "Sleep, or you'll be the next one to be admitted," came a calm voice from behind him. He turned to see the doctor again, her face unfamiliar. Ishan prided himself on remembering faces instantly, but this one eluded him. He recognized her by her voice. As he continued to stare at her, struggling to understand, she guided him toward Hardik.
